Wholesale Level: recall stops at wholesalers/distributors. Distribution Center: recall stops at company distribution centers before reaching trade.

Estimated total financial impact of the recall including product cost, logistics, customer credits, disposal, regulatory fines, and brand impact. Expressed in reporting currency. Used for financial reserves and management reporting.
reference: SOX Financial Reporting, Internal Cost Accounting

description: Latest expiration or best-by date printed on recalled products. Defines the full date range for consumer identification.
reference: FDA Labeling Requirements, Consumer Product Identification

description: Earliest expiration or best-by date printed on recalled products. Helps consumers and retailers identify affected units on shelf.
reference: FDA Labeling Requirements, Consumer Product Identification

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated financial impact amount. Enables multi-currency recall cost tracking and consolidation.
FDA or internal assessment of the health risk posed by the recalled product. Describes potential adverse health consequences and likelihood of occurrence. Used to determine recall classification.
Date when the recall was officially initiated by the company. This is the business event date when the decision to recall was made and communicated internally.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7, Internal Recall SOP

description: Timestamp when this recall event record was last updated. Tracks data currency and supports change audit requirements.
reference: SOX Audit Trail Requirements, Internal Data Governance

description: Latest manufacturing date of products included in the recall scope. Defines the cutoff for affected production runs.
reference: GMP Manufacturing Records, Batch Genealogy Standards

description: Earliest manufacturing date of products included in the recall scope. Used to identify affected inventory based on production timeline.
reference: GMP Manufacturing Records, Batch Genealogy Standards

description: Date when the regulatory authority (FDA, CPSC, EPA, or other governing body) was formally notified of the recall event. Required for compliance reporting.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7.46, CPSC Section 15(b) Reporting

description: Date when the recall was publicly announced through press release, FDA website posting, or other public communication channels.
reference: FDA Recall Communications, FTC Advertising Guidelines

description: Total quantity of affected product that was distributed into the market prior to recall initiation. Expressed in base unit of measure (units, cases, kilograms). Represents the maximum potential recall scope.
Total quantity of product subject to the recall action. May differ from quantity distributed if recall scope is narrowed to specific lots, channels, or geographies.
Actual quantity of recalled product recovered from the market through returns, destructions, and corrections. Updated throughout recall lifecycle. Used to calculate recall effectiveness percentage.
reference: Recall Effectiveness Checks, FDA Audit Trail

description: Unit of measure for all quantity fields in this recall record. Ensures consistent interpretation of distributed, recalled, and recovered quantities.
reference: GS1 Unit of Measure Standards, Internal UOM Master

description: Detailed narrative explanation of the specific defect, contamination, safety issue, or regulatory violation that triggered the recall. Includes root cause findings and quality investigation summary.
reference: FDA Recall Strategy, Internal Quality Management System

value_regex: Class I|Class II|Class III|Voluntary Withdrawal|Market Withdrawal|Stock Recovery
description: FDA or regulatory classification of the recall severity. Class I: dangerous or defective products that could cause serious health problems or death. Class II: products that might cause temporary health problem or pose slight threat of serious nature. Class III: products unlikely to cause adverse health reaction but violate FDA labeling or manufacturing regulations. Voluntary Withdrawal: firm removes product from market not required by FDA. Market Withdrawal: minor violation not subject to legal action. Stock Recovery: correction of product still in distribution chain.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7, CPSC Recall Handbook

description: Calculated percentage of recalled product successfully recovered from the market. Formula: (quantity_recovered / quantity_recalled) * 100. FDA expects effectiveness checks at 2, 4, and 8 weeks. High effectiveness (>95%) supports recall closure.
External recall identification number assigned by the company or regulatory authority for tracking and communication purposes.
reference: FDA Recall Enterprise System (RES), CPSC Recall Guidelines

description: Distribution channel(s) affected by the recall. Retail: products sold through retail stores. Wholesale: products in wholesale/distributor inventory. DTC: direct-to-consumer shipments. Food Service: products sold to restaurants/institutions. Healthcare: products distributed to healthcare facilities. All Channels: recall spans all distribution paths.
reference: Internal Channel Management, Supply Chain Segmentation

description: Specific customer segments, account types, or customer groups affected by the recall. Used for targeted notification and recovery when recall does not affect all customers.
reference: CRM Customer Segmentation, Account Management Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the recall event. Initiated: recall has been declared and communication started. In Progress: active recovery and customer notification underway. Ongoing: recall activities continuing. Completed: all recall activities finished, awaiting final regulatory closure. Terminated: recall ended by firm decision. Closed: regulatory authority has reviewed and closed the recall.
reference: FDA Recall Procedures, Internal Recall Management Process

description: Documented strategy for executing the recall including communication plan, recovery method, depth of recall (consumer level, retail level, wholesale level), and timeline. Required by FDA 21 CFR 7.42.

Primary reason category for the recall event. Safety: product poses health or safety risk. Quality: product fails quality specifications. Regulatory: non-compliance with regulations. Labeling: incorrect or missing label information. Contamination: presence of foreign material or microbial contamination. Allergen: undeclared allergen present.
reference: FDA Recall Guidelines, ISO 22000, GMP Standards

description: Primary regulatory authority overseeing this recall. FDA: U.S. Food and Drug Administration. CPSC: Consumer Product Safety Commission. EPA: Environmental Protection Agency. EU REACH: European chemicals regulation. Health Canada: Canadian health authority. ANVISA: Brazilian health authority. TGA: Australian Therapeutic Goods Administration. MHRA: UK Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|CPSC|EPA|EU REACH|Health Canada|ANVISA|TGA|MHRA|Other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA, CPSC, EPA, International Regulatory Bodies

description: Official case or reference number assigned by the regulatory authority for tracking this recall in their system. Used for all regulatory correspondence and reporting.
reference: FDA Recall Enterprise System, CPSC Case Management

description: Summary of the root cause investigation findings including failure mode, contributing factors, and systemic issues identified. Supports CAPA and regulatory reporting.

business_glossary_term: Recall Lot Scope - Lot Batch Id
description: Foreign key linking to the specific lot or batch record included in this recall scope

Specification of the lot numbers, batch codes, or serial number ranges included in the recall. Critical for traceability and targeted recovery. May be expressed as ranges, lists, or patterns. It cannot support proper lot-level traceability, per-lot quantity tracking, or regulatory reporting. Its functional purpose is entirely replaced by the recall_lot_scope association table, which provides a proper FK-based enumeration of affected lots with per-lot recovery attributes. This field should be deprecated and removed from recall_event once recall_lot_scope is populated.]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 117 (FSMA Traceability), GMP Batch Record Requirements

description: The current recovery status of this specific lot/batch within the recall action. Tracks per-lot progress independently of the overall recall event status. Pending: recovery not yet initiated for this lot; In Progress: recovery activities underway; Recovered: full recovery achieved; Destroyed: product destroyed in lieu of return; Not Located: product cannot be traced.

The quantity of product from this specific lot/batch that is subject to the recall action. May differ from the lot's total quantity_produced if only a portion was distributed or affected. Required for per-lot FDA effectiveness reporting.

The actual quantity of product from this specific lot/batch that has been recovered from the market through returns, destruction, or retrieval. Used to calculate per-lot recovery effectiveness rate for regulatory reporting.

The date on which recovery activities for this specific lot/batch were completed. Null if recovery is not yet complete. Used to calculate time-to-recovery per lot and to support FDA effectiveness check submissions.
