description: Primary street address line including house/building number and street name. Core component of the consumer's physical location used for DTC order fulfillment, direct mail marketing, and GDPR/CCPA data residency compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; USPS Postal Addressing Standards; GS1 GLN Address Standard

description: Secondary address line for apartment, suite, unit, floor, or building designations. Supplements address_line_1 to ensure complete and accurate delivery routing for DTC shipments.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; USPS Postal Addressing Standards

description: Tertiary address line for additional location details such as neighborhood, district, or care-of designations. Used in international address formats where three address lines are required.
reference: GDPR Article 4; Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 International Postal Address Components

description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the consumer's address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used alongside latitude for geocoded proximity analysis, store locator features, and last-mile delivery optimization.
reference: WGS84 Geodetic Datum; ISO 6709 Geographic Point Location

description: The four-digit USPS ZIP+4 extension code that narrows delivery to a specific block face or building. Enables postal presort discounts for direct mail campaigns and improves delivery precision for DTC shipments within the United States.
reference: USPS ZIP+4 Code Standard; USPS Publication 28; USPS CASS Certification

description: ZIP or postal code of the consumer's address. Used for last-mile delivery routing, trade area analysis, demographic segmentation, and carrier rate determination in DTC fulfillment.
reference: USPS ZIP Code Standard; UPU S42; GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140

description: Classification of the address as residential or commercial. Impacts carrier surcharge calculations (UPS/FedEx residential delivery fees), marketing segmentation, and DTC fulfillment cost modeling.
reference: USPS CASS Residential Delivery Indicator; UPS/FedEx Residential Surcharge Guidelines

description: Internal sales territory or region code assigned to this address based on geographic boundaries. Used for Sales Force Automation (SFA) territory assignment, trade promotion planning, and regional sales performance reporting.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud - Territory Management; SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Territory

description: The native record identifier from the originating operational system of record (e.g., Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Contact Address ID, SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Address GUID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing in the Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BP Address GUID; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Address Object; Oracle Cloud ERP Party Site ID

description: Name of the operational system of record from which this address record originated. Supports data lineage, master data management (MDM) conflict resolution, and Silver Layer data quality governance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud; Oracle Cloud ERP; ISO/IEC 27001 Asset Management

description: The full address formatted as a single standardized string per USPS/UPU postal standards after address validation and normalization (e.g., '123 MAIN ST APT 4B, CHICAGO IL 60601-1234'). Used as the canonical address for carrier label generation and deduplication.
reference: USPS CASS Standardization; UPU S42; GDPR Article 5 (Data Accuracy)

description: State, province, or administrative region of the consumer's address using ISO 3166-2 subdivision codes (e.g., US-CA, CA-ON). Supports tax jurisdiction determination, regional compliance, and territory-based sales reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-2 Country Subdivision Codes; GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140

description: IANA time zone identifier for the address location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Used for scheduling personalized marketing communications at optimal local times and for regulatory compliance with time-sensitive consumer notifications.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; GDPR Article 12 (Timely Communication)

description: Date and time when this address record was last modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline, data freshness monitoring, and GDPR data accuracy compliance tracking.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Accuracy); ISO/IEC 27001 Change Management; SAP S/4HANA Change Document

description: Name of the third-party address validation service used to verify this address (e.g., USPS CASS, SmartyStreets, Loqate, Google Maps API). Supports audit trails for data quality governance and vendor performance tracking.
reference: USPS CASS Certification; ISO 9001 Data Quality Management

description: Current validation state of the address record as determined by address verification services (e.g., USPS CASS, SmartyStreets, Loqate). Validated addresses reduce failed DTC deliveries and return-to-sender costs. Failed addresses require consumer outreach before order fulfillment.
reference: USPS CASS (Coding Accuracy Support System); GS1 Address Validation Guidelines

description: Date and time when the address was last validated by the address verification service. Used to determine if re-validation is needed based on data freshness policies and to support data quality SLA reporting.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; USPS CASS Certification Requirements

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each consent record in the Consumer Goods consumer privacy system. Primary key for the consent_record data product, serving as the SSOT for GDPR Article 7 and CCPA compliance audits.

Reference to the marketing or re-consent campaign that prompted this consent record creation or update. Enables campaign-level consent conversion tracking and supports Trade Promotion Management (TPM) and consumer engagement analytics.
reference: GDPR Article 7; FTC Advertising and Labeling Guidelines

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Consent records are captured to satisfy specific regulatory compliance obligations (GDPR Art.6, CCPA §1798.100). Regulatory affairs teams must trace which compliance obligation mandates each consent t
Foreign key linking to regulatory.jurisdiction. Business justification: Data privacy compliance mandates mapping each consent record to its governing jurisdiction for regulatory reporting.

Reference to the Consumer Goods brand for which this consent was captured. Enables brand-level consent reporting and ensures that consent granted for one brand is not incorrectly applied to another brand within the same corporate portfolio.
reference: GDPR Article 7; CCPA Section 1798.100; FTC Advertising and Labeling Guidelines

description: Reference to the consumer profile record for whom this consent record applies. Links the consent record to the consumer master in the CRM/DTC system (Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud).

Channel or mechanism through which the consumer's consent was captured. Identifies whether consent was obtained via a web form, mobile app, in-store interaction, call center agent, paper form, or email link. Required for GDPR proof-of-consent obligations.
reference: GDPR Article 7(1); GDPR Recital 42; CCPA Section 1798.100

description: Exact date and time (with timezone offset) when the consumer's consent action was recorded in the system. This is the principal business event timestamp for the consent record and is immutable once set. Required for GDPR Article 7 audit trail.

The business reason or event that triggered the most recent change to the consent status. Distinguishes consumer-initiated changes from system-driven expirations, administrative overrides, legal requests (e.g., right to erasure), or re-consent campaigns.
reference: GDPR Article 7(3); GDPR Article 17; CCPA Section 1798.105; ISO 29101

description: ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) of the language in which the consent notice was presented to the consumer (e.g., 'en', 'fr', 'de', 'es-MX'). Required to confirm consent was given in an intelligible form per GDPR Article 7.
reference: GDPR Article 7(2); GDPR Recital 42; ISO 639-1; ISO 3166-1

description: Reference identifier or URL pointing to the stored proof-of-consent artifact (e.g., screenshot, signed form scan, call recording reference, Veeva Vault document ID). Supports GDPR Article 7(1) obligation to demonstrate that consent was given.
reference: GDPR Article 7(1); Veeva Vault (Quality Documents); ISO 27001

description: Defines the breadth of the consent grant — whether it applies globally across all Consumer Goods brands and products, is restricted to a specific brand, product category, or a single campaign. Enables granular consent management across the product portfolio.
reference: GDPR Article 7; GDPR Recital 32; CCPA Section 1798.100

description: Current lifecycle status of the consent record indicating whether the consumer has actively granted, withdrawn, or is pending confirmation of consent, or whether the consent has expired past its validity period.

Verbatim text or reference hash of the consent notice, privacy policy excerpt, or opt-in language presented to the consumer at the time of consent capture. Immutable record of what the consumer agreed to, required for GDPR Article 7 proof-of-consent.
reference: GDPR Article 7(1); GDPR Recital 42; CCPA Section 1798.100

description: Category of consent being captured, defining the specific processing activity for which the consumer has provided or withheld consent. Covers marketing email, SMS, push notification, data sharing, profiling, and cookies as required by GDPR and CCPA.
reference: GDPR Article 6; GDPR Article 22; CCPA Section 1798.120; ePrivacy Directive

description: Version identifier of the consent notice, privacy policy, or terms document presented to the consumer at the time of consent capture. Enables tracking of which policy version was in effect when consent was given, critical for GDPR Article 7 compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 7(1); GDPR Recital 42; CCPA Section 1798.100

description: Comma-separated list of cookie categories for which the consumer has granted consent (e.g., 'functional,analytics,marketing'). Captures granular cookie preferences as required by the ePrivacy Directive and GDPR for digital channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: necessary|functional|analytics|marketing|social_media|personalization|advertising — promote to reference product]
reference: ePrivacy Directive Article 5(3); GDPR Article 6; CCPA Section 1798.120; IAB TCF 2.0

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the consumer's country of residence at the time of consent capture. Determines applicable privacy regulation (e.g., GDPR for EU countries, CCPA for USA/California). Critical for multi-jurisdiction compliance management.

Timestamp when this consent record was first created in the Consumer Goods data platform (Silver Layer). Distinct from capture_timestamp which records the business event time. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.
Type of device used by the consumer when providing consent in digital channels. Supports forensic audit trails and UX analysis of consent capture flows.

Indicates whether the consumer completed a double opt-in confirmation process (e.g., clicked a confirmation link in a verification email) in addition to the initial consent action. Double opt-in provides stronger proof of consent for marketing communications under GDPR.
reference: GDPR Recital 32; GDPR Article 7; ePrivacy Directive Article 13

description: Exact date and time when the consumer completed the double opt-in confirmation step. Null if double opt-in was not required or not yet completed. Provides the definitive timestamp for consent activation in double opt-in workflows.
reference: GDPR Recital 32; GDPR Article 7; ePrivacy Directive Article 13

description: Date from which this consent record is considered active and binding. Typically aligns with the capture date but may differ for backdated or scheduled consent activations. Supports MASTER_AGREEMENT role effective-period tracking.

Date on which this consent record expires and must be renewed or re-confirmed by the consumer. Null indicates open-ended consent with no defined expiry. Used to trigger consent renewal workflows and ensure ongoing compliance with GDPR's requirement for time-limited consent.

IP address of the device from which the consumer submitted their consent. Captured for digital consent channels (web form, mobile app, email link) as evidence of consent origin. May be considered PII under GDPR in some EU jurisdictions.
reference: GDPR Article 4(1); GDPR Recital 30; CCPA Section 1798.140(o)

description: Indicates whether this row represents the current active consent record for the consumer-consent_type combination. Supports Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD Type 2) pattern in the Silver Layer, where historical consent versions are retained for audit purposes and this flag identifies the latest version.
reference: GDPR Article 7; ISO 29101; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer conventions

description: The lawful basis under GDPR Article 6 or equivalent regulation for processing the consumer's personal data. Consent is one of six legal bases; this field distinguishes consent-based processing from other lawful bases to support compliance reporting.
reference: GDPR Article 6; GDPR Recital 40-49; CCPA Section 1798.100

description: Age threshold (in years) used to determine whether parental consent was required for this consent record, based on the applicable jurisdiction's rules (e.g., 16 for GDPR default, 13 for COPPA, 13-16 for EU member state variations). Null if consumer is not a minor.

Indicates whether parental or guardian consent was obtained for a minor consumer. Required under GDPR Article 8 for children under 16 (or lower age threshold set by member state) and COPPA for US consumers under 13.
reference: GDPR Article 8; COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act); CCPA Section 1798.120

description: The consent status immediately before the most recent status change. Enables point-in-time audit reconstruction and supports the immutable event log requirement for GDPR Article 7 compliance audits without requiring a separate history table join.

Identifier of the system, application, or human agent (call center representative ID or system name) that processed and recorded the consent event. Supports accountability requirements under GDPR Article 5(2) and provides an audit trail of who or what captured the consent.
reference: GDPR Article 5(2); GDPR Article 24; CCPA Section 1798.100

business_glossary_term: Profiling and Automated Decision-Making Consent Flag
description: Indicates whether the consumer has consented to automated profiling and decision-making activities, including personalization algorithms, NPS scoring, CLTV modeling, and segmentation. Required under GDPR Article 22 for automated decision-making with significant effects.
reference: GDPR Article 22; GDPR Recital 71; CCPA Section 1798.120

description: Date by which the consumer must re-confirm their consent before the current consent record is automatically withdrawn or expired. Null if re-consent is not required. Used to drive automated consent renewal campaigns.
reference: GDPR Article 7; GDPR Recital 42; CCPA Section 1798.100

description: Indicates whether this consent record requires re-confirmation from the consumer due to a material change in the privacy policy, consent notice, or processing purpose. Triggers re-consent outreach workflows in the CRM system.
reference: GDPR Article 7; GDPR Recital 42; CCPA Section 1798.100

description: Sequential version number of this consent record for the consumer-consent_type combination. Increments with each status change or update, enabling full audit history reconstruction. Version 1 is the initial consent capture; subsequent versions reflect changes.

The applicable privacy regulation or jurisdiction framework under which this consent record was created and must be managed. Determines the legal basis, retention rules, and consumer rights applicable to this record (e.g., GDPR for EU consumers, CCPA for California residents).
reference: GDPR Article 3; CCPA Section 1798.140(g); LGPD Article 3; ISO 29101

description: Number of days this consent record must be retained for compliance purposes after the consent is withdrawn or expires. Determined by applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., GDPR Article 5(1)(e) storage limitation, CCPA). Drives automated data lifecycle management.
reference: GDPR Article 5(1)(e); GDPR Article 17; CCPA Section 1798.105; ISO 27001

description: Operational system of record from which this consent record originated (e.g., Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud, SAP S/4HANA, web portal, mobile app). Enables data lineage tracking and system-of-record reconciliation across the Consumer Goods technology landscape. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salesforce_cgc|sap_s4hana|web_portal|mobile_app|call_center_crm|in_store_pos|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Indicates whether the consumer has explicitly consented to their personal data being shared with third-party partners (e.g., retail partners, data brokers, analytics providers). Distinct from general data sharing consent; specifically governs external data transfers.
reference: GDPR Article 6; GDPR Article 44-49 (International Transfers); CCPA Section 1798.120; EU REACH Regulation

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this consent record in the data platform. Used for incremental data processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.

Channel or mechanism through which the consumer withdrew their consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Required to demonstrate that withdrawal was as easy as giving consent per GDPR Article 7(3).

Exact date and time when the consumer withdrew their consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. GDPR Article 7(3) requires that withdrawal be as easy as giving consent; this timestamp documents the withdrawal event for audit purposes.
Unique surrogate identifier for the household record in the Consumer Goods lakehouse. Primary key for all household-level analytics, loyalty pooling, and basket analysis.

Foreign key linking to consumer.address. Business justification: A household has a physical address (dwelling location) that is the authoritative geographic reference for the household unit. The address table is the SSOT for physical addresses in the consumer domai
Reference to the source household record that was merged into this household during deduplication or household consolidation. Null if no merge has occurred. Supports data lineage and audit trails.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud CRM; GDPR Article 5 (Data Accuracy)

description: Indicates whether the household has demonstrated strong brand loyalty to the company's portfolio based on repeat purchase patterns and SOV (Share of Voice) analysis. Used for brand marketing investment decisions.
reference: Nielsen IQ Retail Measurement; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Account Management

description: Indicates whether the household has exercised the right to opt out of the sale of personal information under CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act). Mandatory compliance field for California-resident households.
Indicates whether children under 18 are present in the household. Key trigger for cross-sell targeting of baby, child health, and family-oriented CPG/FMCG product categories.
reference: Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel Life Stage Segmentation; COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act)

description: Categorical CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) band assigned to the household based on historical purchase behavior and predictive modeling. Used for investment prioritization in trade promotion and personalization.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Account Management

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to any consent or privacy preference for the household. Critical for GDPR/CCPA audit trails and demonstrating lawful basis for data processing.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country of the household's primary residence. Used for regulatory compliance (GDPR, CCPA) and international market segmentation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 3; CCPA Section 1798.140

description: Timestamp when the household record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and GDPR/CCPA compliance reporting.
reference: GDPR Article 5; ISO 8601; SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail

description: Originating system or channel from which the household record was first created or last enriched. Used for data lineage, quality scoring, and source system reconciliation in the lakehouse. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crm|loyalty_program|ecommerce|panel|pos|dtc|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud CRM; TradeEdge

description: Indicates whether the household actively engages with the brand through digital channels (e-commerce, mobile app, social media). Used for DTC (Direct to Consumer) channel strategy and omnichannel personalization.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Retail Execution; GDPR Article 6

description: Date on which the household was dissolved, merged, or deactivated. Null for active households. Used for lifecycle reporting and GDPR/CCPA data retention compliance.
Type of residential dwelling occupied by the household. Used for product category targeting (e.g., home care, cleaning products) and basket size normalization in CPG analytics.
Estimated number of individuals residing in the household. Used for basket size normalization, per-capita consumption analysis, and product volume forecasting in S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning).
reference: Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel; SAP IBP Demand Planning

description: Date on which the household record was first established in the system, representing when the household unit was recognized as a distinct entity for loyalty and analytics purposes.

Indicates whether the household has provided valid consent for data processing under GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation). Mandatory for EU-resident households before any marketing or analytics processing.
Broad geographic region of the household's residence (e.g., Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, West, Southwest). Used for regional demand planning and distribution channel management.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning; Nielsen IQ Market Intelligence

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the household, used in CRM, loyalty, and DTC channel communications. Distinct from the surrogate primary key.
reference: GS1 Global Location Number (GLN) Standards; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud CRM

description: Human-readable label for the household, typically the primary shopper's surname or a family name used for display in CRM and loyalty program communications.
Current lifecycle state of the household record. 'merged' indicates consolidation with another household; 'dissolved' indicates the household no longer exists as a unit.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud CRM; GDPR Article 17 (Right to Erasure)

description: Categorical classification of the household composition used for segmentation and cross-sell targeting in CPG/FMCG analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single|couple|family|multi_generational|shared_living|other — promote to reference product if values expand]
reference: Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel Segmentation Standards

description: Estimated annual household income bracket used for consumer segmentation, RSP (Recommended Selling Price) sensitivity analysis, and premium product targeting. Derived from third-party panel data or self-reported.
reference: Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel Income Segmentation; CCPA Section 1798.140

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the household record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer and data freshness monitoring.
reference: GDPR Article 5; ISO 8601; Databricks Delta Lake CDC

description: Life stage classification of the household used for targeted marketing and NPD (New Product Development) insights (e.g., young_single, young_couple_no_children, family_with_young_children, family_with_teens, empty_nester, retired). Enables cross-sell triggers such as recommending baby products when life stage transitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: young_single|young_couple_no_children|family_with_young_children|family_with_teens|empty_nester|retired|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel Life Stage Segmentation; SAP IBP Demand Planning Segmentation

description: Date on which the household was enrolled in the loyalty program. Used to calculate loyalty tenure, tier progression timelines, and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) cohort analysis.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Trade Promotion

description: Current unredeemed loyalty points balance accumulated at the household level across all member consumers. Supports household-level pooling for CPG/FMCG loyalty programs.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Trade Promotion; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Current loyalty program tier of the household based on cumulative purchase activity. Drives reward multipliers, exclusive offers, and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) segmentation.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Trade Promotion; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Strategic consumer market segment assigned to the household for brand marketing and consumer engagement programs (e.g., health_conscious, value_seeker, premium_buyer, eco_friendly). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: health_conscious|value_seeker|premium_buyer|eco_friendly|convenience_driven|brand_loyal — promote to reference product]
reference: Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel Segmentation; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Account Management

description: Indicates whether the household has opted in to receive marketing communications (email, SMS, push notifications) from the brand. Governs eligibility for trade promotion and consumer engagement campaigns.
reference: GDPR Article 6; CCPA Section 1798.120; FTC CAN-SPAM Act

description: Most recent NPS (Net Promoter Score) recorded for the household, ranging from 0 to 10. Used to classify households as Promoters (9-10), Passives (7-8), or Detractors (0-6) for consumer engagement programs.
reference: NPS Methodology (Bain & Company); Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud CRM

description: Date on which the most recent NPS (Net Promoter Score) survey response was recorded for the household. Used to assess recency of satisfaction data for consumer engagement decisions.
reference: NPS Methodology (Bain & Company); Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud CRM

description: Indicates whether the household is an active member of a consumer research panel (e.g., Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel). Panel households provide purchase diary data used for market intelligence and SOM (Share of Market) analysis.
reference: Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel; ISO 20252 (Market Research Standards)

description: Indicates whether the household owns pets. Used for targeted marketing of pet care product categories and cross-sell recommendations within the CPG/FMCG portfolio.
reference: Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Account Management

description: BCP 47 language tag representing the household's preferred communication language (e.g., en-US, es-MX, fr-FR). Used for localized marketing content, product labeling compliance, and consumer engagement personalization.
reference: BCP 47 Language Tag Standard; GDPR Article 12 (Transparent Communication); FTC Advertising Standards

description: The dominant channel through which the household makes purchases. Used for DTC (Direct to Consumer) strategy, channel mix analysis, and trade promotion targeting.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Retail Execution; TradeEdge Channel Visibility

description: The retail banner or chain where the household conducts the majority of its grocery or consumer goods shopping (e.g., Walmart, Target, Kroger). Used for OSA (On Shelf Availability) and POG (Planogram) analytics.
reference: Nielsen IQ Retail Measurement; TradeEdge Secondary Sales

description: Indicates whether the household regularly purchases private label or store-brand products in addition to or instead of branded CPG products. Used for competitive analysis and brand switching risk assessment.
reference: Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel; TradeEdge Channel Visibility

description: Categorical band representing how frequently the household makes purchases across CPG/FMCG categories. Derived from POS (Point of Sale) transaction history and used for demand sensing and replenishment planning.
reference: TradeEdge Demand Sensing; Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel

description: Indicates whether the household owns or rents their residence. Used for home improvement and home care product category targeting and consumer segmentation.
Unique surrogate identifier for each consumer preference record in the Consumer Goods lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the preference data product.

Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Consumer preference records in CPG capture product category preferences (e.g., 'prefers hair care over skin care') used in personalization, segment assignment, and category-level marketing. preference

Reference to the formal consent record that authorizes the collection and use of this preference. Links to the consent management system to ensure every preference is backed by a valid, auditable consent event as required by GDPR and CCPA.
reference: GDPR Article 7; CCPA Section 1798.100; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud — Consent Management

description: Reference to the household entity this consumer belongs to, enabling household-level preference aggregation for CPG targeting and personalization programs.
reference: Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel — Household Identifier

description: Reference to the CPG brand for which this preference applies. Enables brand-level preference overrides and brand-specific personalization within a multi-brand portfolio.
Reference to the consumer profile who owns this preference record. Links to the consumer master in the CRM/DTC system (Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud).
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud — Consumer Profile Object

description: Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: CPG personalization and recommendation engines track shopper preferences at SKU level (specific variant, scent, pack size). preference.sku_preference is a plain denormalized field. Adding sku_id FK en

Indicates whether this preference record has been overridden at the brand level, superseding the consumer's global preference. When True, the brand_id field identifies which brand applied the override. Enables brand-specific preference management within a multi-brand CPG portfolio.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud — Brand Preference Management

description: The business reason for the brand-level preference override when brand_override_flag is True. Used for audit trails and compliance reporting, particularly for regulatory restrictions (e.g., FDA, CPSC product safety communications).
reference: FDA Regulatory Compliance; CPSC Consumer Safety Notifications

description: The consumer's CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) tier at the time this preference was recorded. Enables preference prioritization and investment decisions — high-CLTV consumers may receive more personalized preference-driven experiences and premium engagement programs.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud — CLTV Segmentation; Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel

description: Numeric confidence level (0.0000 to 1.0000) assigned to this preference record, particularly relevant for ML-inferred preferences. A score of 1.0 indicates absolute certainty (typically for declared preferences); lower scores indicate inferred preferences with varying degrees of model confidence. Used by recommendation systems to weight preference signals.
reference: CPG AI/ML Personalization Standards; Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel Analytics

description: The GDPR lawful basis under which this preference data is collected and processed. 'explicit_consent' is the most common basis for CPG consumer preference programs. Required for GDPR Article 6 compliance documentation.
The ISO 8601 timestamp when this preference record was first captured in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp and is used for GDPR consent audit trails and data lineage tracking.

Indicates whether this preference record is subject to an active Data Subject Request (DSR) under GDPR or CCPA (e.g., right to erasure, right to access, right to portability). When True, downstream systems must apply appropriate data handling restrictions.

The date from which this preference record becomes valid and actionable for personalization, communication orchestration, and recommendation systems. Aligns with GDPR consent effective date requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 7; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud — Consent Management

description: The date after which this preference record is no longer valid. Nullable for open-ended preferences. Used to enforce time-bound consent periods and seasonal preference windows. When expired, preference_status transitions to 'expired'.

The consumer's preferred flavor (for food/beverage/oral care) or fragrance (for personal care/household products). Drives variant-level recommendations and new product development (NPD) consumer insights. Aligns with INCI nomenclature for fragrance ingredients.
reference: INCI — International Nomenclature of Cosmetic Ingredients; IFRA Standards

description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the geographic jurisdiction in which this preference is valid. Enables country-specific preference management for global CPG brands operating under different regulatory frameworks (e.g., GDPR in EU, CCPA in USA).

The data signal or behavioral evidence used to infer this preference when preference_source is ML-inferred. Provides transparency into the inference methodology for model explainability and GDPR Article 22 automated decision-making compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 22 — Automated Decision-Making; Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel

description: The version identifier of the ML model used to infer this preference when preference_source is 'inferred_ml' or 'inferred_purchase_history'. Enables model governance, A/B testing of recommendation algorithms, and reproducibility of inferred preferences for audit purposes.
reference: CPG AI/ML Model Governance Standards; ISO 9001 — Quality Management

description: Indicates whether this preference applies globally across all brands in the CPG portfolio (True) or is specific to a single brand identified by brand_id (False). Global preferences serve as the default when no brand-specific preference exists.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud — Multi-Brand Preference Management

description: The ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent update to this preference record, whether from a consumer-declared change, a system re-inference, or an administrative override. Critical for freshness scoring in personalization engines and GDPR audit trails.

Free-text field for additional context or qualifications about this preference record, such as special instructions captured during a call-center interaction or notes from a consumer survey. Used by CRM agents and consumer engagement teams.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud — Consumer Notes

description: The consumer's NPS (Net Promoter Score) segment classification at the time this preference was captured. Used to contextualize preference data — promoters may have stronger brand preferences while detractors may have channel avoidance preferences. Supports consumer engagement strategy.
reference: NPS Methodology — Bain & Company; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud — Consumer Insights

description: Indicates whether the consumer has opted in (True) or opted out (False) for this specific preference category. Critical for GDPR/CCPA compliance — a False value means the consumer must NOT be targeted via this preference dimension.

The consumer's preferred pack size or format (e.g., 'travel_size', 'standard', 'family_size', 'bulk', 'subscription_bundle'). Used to tailor product recommendations and promotional offers to the consumer's typical purchase behavior.
reference: GS1 GTIN Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM — Packaging Hierarchy

description: High-level classification of the preference type. Defines what dimension of consumer behavior or communication this preference record governs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: product_category|brand|flavor_fragrance|pack_size|channel|communication_channel|contact_frequency|preferred_time_of_day|preferred_language — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud — Consumer Preference Framework

description: Ordinal ranking of this preference value relative to other values within the same category for the same consumer. Rank 1 indicates the most preferred option. Enables ranked preference lists for recommendation engines (e.g., first, second, third flavor choice).

Origin of the preference record indicating whether it was explicitly declared by the consumer through a digital or physical touchpoint, or inferred by an ML model or behavioral analysis. description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: DTC order line revenue, discounts, and COGS post to specific GL accounts. Consumer goods revenue accounting requires line-level GL coding for product category P&L, trade promotion deductions, and net
Reference to the outbound shipment record associated with this order line. Enables traceability from order line to physical shipment for OTIF tracking and logistics cost allocation.
reference: Blue Yonder WMS - Shipment; SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Delivery

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: DTC order lines must be traceable to specific lot/batch for product traceability, recall response, and shelf-life compliance reporting. Consumer goods regulatory requirements (e.g., FDA, EU traceabili

Foreign key linking to distribution.pick_task. Business justification: Each DTC order line drives a specific warehouse pick task. Linking them enables pick accuracy reporting per order line, exception management (short picks, substitutions), and fulfillment SLA tracking
Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_recall. Business justification: Recall effectiveness analysis needs to associate each order line with the recall that affected the SKU.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Each line item contributes to a profit center; needed for profit‑center reporting per SKU.
Reference to the end consumer or shopper who placed the DTC order. Enables consumer-level purchase history, CLTV analysis, and personalization. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE PARTY_REFERENCE category.

Reference to the product SKU purchased on this line. Enables SKU-level purchase analysis, product affinity modeling, and replenishment triggers. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE RESOURCE_REFERENCE category.
reference: GS1 Global Data Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_movement. Business justification: Shipment tracking process ties each order line to the inventory movement record that shipped the product.
Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Fulfillment allocation report requires linking each order line to the specific stock position that supplied the SKU.
Reference to the consumer subscription record that triggered this order line, when subscription_flag is True. Enables subscription lifecycle analysis and recurring revenue reporting.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud - Subscription Management

description: Foreign key linking to supply.atp_record. Business justification: REQUIRED: Order line allocation references the ATP record used for inventory commitment; this link supports order‑to‑inventory reconciliation and fulfillment reporting.
Date on which the order line was confirmed as delivered to the consumer. Used for OTIF calculation, consumer satisfaction analysis, and carrier performance reporting.
reference: Blue Yonder WMS - Proof of Delivery; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: Date on which the order line was physically dispatched from the fulfillment warehouse. Used to calculate On Time In Full (OTIF) performance and carrier SLA adherence.

description: Code identifying the brand under which the product on this order line is marketed. Supports brand-level revenue reporting, Share of Market (SOM) analysis, and brand portfolio performance tracking.

description: Identifies the specific DTC sub-channel through which this order line was placed (e.g., brand website, mobile app, subscription program, social commerce). Supports channel-level revenue attribution and consumer journey analytics.

description: Standard cost of goods for the units on this order line at time of sale. Used for gross margin calculation, DTC channel profitability analysis, and EBITDA reporting. Classified confidential as it reveals internal cost structure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Standard Cost; GAAP ASC 330; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Timestamp when this order line record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data lineage and SOX compliance.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary amounts on this order line are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency DTC operations and financial consolidation.

description: Current fulfillment lifecycle state of this order line. Tracks progression from order confirmation through warehouse picking, packing, shipment, and delivery. Supports On Time In Full (OTIF) monitoring and consumer communication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|confirmed|picking|packed|shipped|delivered|cancelled|backordered — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Delivery Status; Blue Yonder WMS - Order Status

description: Indicates whether this order line was designated as a gift by the consumer. True when the consumer selected gift options (e.g., gift wrapping, gift message). Supports gifting program analytics and packaging operations.

description: GS1-standard Global Trade Item Number for the product on this line. Enables cross-retailer product identification, barcode scanning, and regulatory traceability. Conforms to GS1 GTIN-8, GTIN-12 (UPC), GTIN-13 (EAN), or GTIN-14 formats.
reference: GS1 Global Data Standards - GTIN Specification

description: Indicates whether the product on this order line is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, packaging, or shipping restrictions. True for products subject to DOT, IATA, or EPA hazmat regulations. Critical for carrier compliance and Safety Data Sheet (SDS) requirements.
reference: EPA Hazardous Materials Regulations; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 (SDS); DOT 49 CFR

description: Indicates whether this order line has been subject to a consumer return. True when a return has been initiated or completed. Enables return rate analysis by SKU, category, and consumer segment for product quality and consumer experience programs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Returns Order; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud

description: Total monetary discount applied to this order line, including promotional discounts, coupon redemptions, and loyalty rewards. Used for Trade Promotion Management (TPM) analysis and margin reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Discount Condition; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud TPM

description: Discount rate applied to this order line expressed as a percentage of the gross unit price. Enables promotional effectiveness analysis and margin impact assessment without requiring price recalculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Discount Percentage; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud

description: Net monetary value of this order line after all discounts have been applied but before tax. Core revenue recognition field for DTC channel P&L reporting and COGS analysis. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT monetary component.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Net Amount; SOX Financial Reporting; GAAP Revenue Recognition ASC 606

description: Sequential position of this line item within the parent DTC order. Used for ordering, display, and reconciliation of multi-line orders. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Sales Order Item Number

description: Gross line value before tax, calculated as unit_price multiplied by quantity_ordered. Represents the pre-discount, pre-tax revenue contribution of this line item.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Net Value; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Final total amount for this order line inclusive of all discounts and taxes. Represents the actual amount charged to the consumer for this line item. Used for revenue reporting and consumer billing reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Total Amount; SOX Financial Reporting; GAAP ASC 606

description: Number of loyalty program points earned by the consumer for purchasing this order line. Supports loyalty program accrual tracking, Consumer Lifetime Value (CLTV) modeling, and consumer engagement analytics.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud - Loyalty Management; CCPA Section 1798.140

description: Number of loyalty program points redeemed by the consumer as a discount on this order line. Enables loyalty redemption rate analysis and program liability management.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud - Loyalty Management

description: Commercial name of the product SKU as displayed to the consumer at time of purchase. Captured at order time to preserve historical accuracy independent of future product name changes.

description: Date communicated to the consumer as the expected delivery date for this order line. Drives consumer expectation management, NPS impact analysis, and carrier performance benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Requested Delivery Date; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud

description: Date on which the order line was committed to ship to the consumer at time of order placement. Basis for Available to Promise (ATP) and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance measurement.

description: Number of units cancelled on this order line, either by the consumer or due to inventory unavailability. Supports cancellation rate analysis and demand signal correction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Cancelled Quantity

description: Number of units of the SKU ordered by the consumer on this line. Basis for demand planning, inventory reservation, and fulfillment processing. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_QUANTITY category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Order Quantity

description: Actual number of units of the SKU dispatched to the consumer for this line. May differ from quantity_ordered due to partial fulfillment, Out of Stock (OOS) situations, or split shipments. Key metric for On Time In Full (OTIF) measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Delivery Quantity; Blue Yonder WMS

description: Indicates whether this order line is subject to a regulatory hold preventing fulfillment (e.g., FDA product recall, CPSC safety alert, EU REACH restriction). True when a hold is active. Supports compliance-driven order management and consumer safety programs.
reference: FDA 21 CFR; CPSC Consumer Product Safety Act; EU REACH Regulation; Veeva Vault Regulatory

description: Standardized code indicating the reason for the consumer return of this order line. Supports product quality analysis, Quality Control (QC) escalation, and consumer experience improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DEFECTIVE|WRONG_ITEM|NOT_AS_DESCRIBED|CHANGED_MIND|DAMAGED_IN_TRANSIT|LATE_DELIVERY|DUPLICATE_ORDER|OTHER — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Return Reason; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Indicates whether this order line was generated as part of a recurring subscription program (e.g., Subscribe & Save). True for subscription-triggered lines. Enables subscription revenue tracking and replenishment program analysis.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud - Subscription Management

description: Total tax amount applied to this order line, including applicable sales tax, VAT, or GST based on consumer jurisdiction. Required for tax compliance reporting and financial reconciliation.

description: Unit of measure in which the SKU quantity is expressed on this order line (e.g., EA=Each, CS=Case, PK=Pack). Aligns with GS1 and SAP UOM standards for consistent quantity interpretation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PK|BX|KG|LB|OZ|L|ML — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: GS1 Global Data Standards - Unit of Measure; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Gross selling price per unit of the SKU at the time of order placement, before any discounts or promotions. Represents the consumer-facing price (RSP/MSRP or DTC-specific price). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT category.

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this order line record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change data capture (CDC), audit trails, and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); ISO 8601

description: Unique surrogate identifier for a DTC return or refund request record. Primary key of this entity.

Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Return processing workflow needs the original shipment reference to manage reverse logistics, refunds, and OTIF metrics.

Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: DTC returns with quality_defect_code or safety_incident_flag trigger nonconformance records in the quality system. Consumer goods QA teams require this traceability to aggregate consumer-reported defe
Reference to the original DTC order against which this return is raised. Links the return to the parent sales transaction for financial reconciliation and consumer satisfaction tracking.
reference: SAP SD Sales Order; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud

description: Foreign key linking to quality.product_complaint. Business justification: DTC returns with safety_incident_flag or quality_defect_code must be traceable to a formal product complaint record for regulatory adverse event reporting and recall management. Consumer goods regulat

Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_recall. Business justification: Recall management report requires linking each consumer return to the associated product recall record to track recall effectiveness.

Foreign key linking to inventory.recall_event. Business justification: Returns triggered by product recalls must link to the inventory recall_event record for recall effectiveness measurement (quantity recovered vs. distributed) and regulatory closure reporting. dtc_retu
Reference to the consumer profile who initiated the return request. Links to the SSOT consumer master record for CRM, loyalty, and consumer satisfaction analytics.

description: Reference to the product (SKU) being returned. Enables product-level return rate analysis, quality feedback loops, and defect trending by SKU.

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_movement. Business justification: Return movement tracking links each return to the inventory movement that records the inbound return flow.
Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Return processing records which stock position the returned SKU is returned to for inventory reconciliation.
Date on which the return request was approved by the consumer service team or automated system. Used for SLA tracking and return processing time analytics.

description: Customer service case or ticket number associated with this return request in the CRM system. Enables cross-referencing between the return record and the consumer service interaction history in Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud.

description: Post-return consumer satisfaction rating collected via survey (typically 1–5 or 1–10 scale). Used as an input to Net Promoter Score (NPS) calculations and consumer experience improvement programs. Collected via Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud post-return survey.

description: Timestamp when this return record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking. Distinct from request_timestamp which captures the business event time.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); ISO 8601

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this return record (refund_amount, restocking_fee_amount, net_refund_amount). Supports multi-currency DTC operations across global markets.

description: Final disposition decision for the returned product after quality inspection. Drives inventory management, COGS impact, and regulatory compliance for product destruction. Valid values: restock (return to sellable inventory), refurbish (rework required), destroy (product destroyed per policy), return_to_vendor (sent back to supplier), quarantine (held pending investigation).
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; FDA 21 CFR Part 211; SAP QM Usage Decision

description: Expiration or best-before date printed on the returned product. Used for FEFO compliance validation, quality assessment of returned goods, and regulatory reporting for expired product returns.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211; EU REACH Regulation; ISO 22716

description: Indicates whether this return has been flagged for manual fraud review based on fraud_risk_score or other risk signals. Supports loss prevention workflows and return abuse management.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud; FTC Consumer Protection Guidelines

description: Numeric risk score (0.00–100.00) assigned by the fraud detection system to assess the likelihood of return fraud. Drives manual review routing and automated rejection thresholds. Supports loss prevention analytics.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud; FTC Consumer Protection Guidelines

description: Number of loyalty program points credited back to the consumer's account as part of the refund. Populated when refund_method is 'loyalty_points' or when loyalty points were originally redeemed on the order. Supports loyalty program financial reconciliation.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Loyalty Management; CCPA Section 1798.140

description: Net monetary amount actually refunded to the consumer after deducting any restocking fees or adjustments from the gross refund amount. Used for financial settlement and consumer account reconciliation.
reference: SAP FI Credit Memo; Oracle Cloud Financials; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Assessed condition of the returned product upon receipt at the warehouse. Determines disposition (restock, refurbish, destroy) and impacts inventory valuation. Assessed by warehouse quality inspection team per GMP guidelines.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; ISO 22716 GMP for Cosmetics; SAP QM Inspection

description: Standardized quality defect classification code assigned when the return reason indicates a product quality issue (defective, damaged). Feeds into the Quality Management (QM) system for defect trending, root cause analysis, and regulatory reporting. Sourced from SAP QM or Siemens Opcenter MES quality module.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; FDA 21 CFR Part 211; CPSC Product Safety; SAP QM Defect Catalog

description: Date on which the returned product was physically received at the warehouse or returns processing center. Triggers refund processing and inventory restock workflows. Sourced from Blue Yonder WMS goods receipt.
reference: Blue Yonder WMS Goods Receipt; SAP WM Returns Putaway

description: Gross monetary amount to be refunded or already refunded to the consumer, in the transaction currency. Represents the base refund before any deductions such as restocking fees. Used for financial reconciliation and COGS impact reporting.
reference: SAP FI Credit Memo; Oracle Cloud Financials; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Date on which the refund was issued to the consumer. Used for financial reconciliation, Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) adjustment, and consumer satisfaction SLA measurement.
reference: SAP FI Credit Memo; Oracle Cloud Financials; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Method by which the refund is issued to the consumer. Drives financial processing workflows and consumer communication. Valid values: original_payment (refund to original payment instrument), store_credit (credit to consumer account), loyalty_points (converted to loyalty program points), bank_transfer (direct bank transfer), gift_card (issued as gift card value).
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud; SAP FI Payment Processing; CCPA Section 1798.140

description: Indicates whether this return requires submission of a regulatory report to a governing body (FDA, CPSC, EPA). Driven by safety_incident_flag, product category, and jurisdiction. Supports compliance workflow automation and audit readiness.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314; CPSC Consumer Product Safety Act; EPA Reporting; SOX Compliance

description: Date on which the return request was rejected. Populated only when return_status is 'rejected'. Used for consumer communication SLA tracking and dispute resolution analytics.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud; FTC Consumer Protection Guidelines

description: Reason code explaining why the return request was rejected. Populated only when return_status is 'rejected'. Supports consumer dispute resolution and return policy analytics.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud; FTC Consumer Protection Guidelines

description: Calendar date on which the consumer submitted the return request. Used for SLA measurement, return window eligibility validation, and trend reporting.
reference: SAP SD Returns Order; FTC Consumer Protection Guidelines

description: Precise date and time (with timezone offset) when the consumer submitted the return request. Used for SLA measurement at sub-day granularity and audit trail purposes.
Fee deducted from the refund amount to cover reverse logistics and restocking costs, where applicable per return policy. Used for net refund calculation and cost recovery reporting.
reference: SAP SD Pricing Conditions; Oracle Cloud Financials

description: Digital or service channel through which the consumer initiated the return request. Distinct from return method (physical logistics). Used for channel analytics and consumer experience optimization.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud; CCPA Section 1798.140

description: Carrier tracking number assigned to the prepaid return shipping label issued to the consumer. Enables reverse logistics tracking and proof-of-return verification. Sourced from Blue Yonder WMS or carrier integration.
reference: Blue Yonder WMS Transportation; SAP SD Delivery Tracking

description: Physical method by which the consumer returns the product. Determines logistics handling, prepaid label issuance, and reverse logistics cost allocation. Valid values: mail_in (consumer ships via postal/courier), drop_off (consumer drops at designated location), carrier_pickup (carrier collects from consumer address), in_store (returned at retail partner location).
reference: Blue Yonder WMS Reverse Logistics; SAP SD Returns Delivery

description: Externally-visible, human-readable Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) number assigned to this return request. Used in consumer communications, shipping labels, and customer service interactions. Sourced from Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud or SAP SD returns order.
reference: SAP SD Returns Order; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud

description: Version identifier of the return policy that was in effect at the time the return was requested. Ensures consistent policy application and supports audit trails for policy change impact analysis.

description: Number of units of the product being returned in this return request. Used for inventory restock planning, refund calculation, and return rate KPI computation.

description: Standardized reason code explaining why the consumer is returning the product. Drives quality feedback loops, product improvement, and carrier claims. Valid values: defective (product malfunction), wrong_item (incorrect item shipped), changed_mind (consumer preference change), damaged_in_transit (carrier damage), not_as_described (product mismatch), missing_parts (incomplete product). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: defective|wrong_item|changed_mind|damaged_in_transit|not_as_described|missing_parts — promote to reference product]

description: Free-text narrative provided by the consumer describing the specific reason for the return. Supplements the standardized return reason code with qualitative detail for quality analysis and consumer service resolution.

description: Current lifecycle state of the return request. Tracks the return workflow from initial consumer request through final resolution. Valid values: requested (consumer submitted), approved (return authorized), received (item received at warehouse), refunded (refund issued), rejected (return denied), cancelled (consumer cancelled). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|approved|received|refunded|rejected|cancelled — promote to reference product]

description: Indicates whether the return request was submitted within the brand's published return policy window (e.g., 30 days from delivery). Drives automated approval/rejection workflows and consumer communication.

description: Indicates whether the return involves a consumer safety incident (e.g., injury, adverse reaction, product hazard). When true, triggers mandatory regulatory reporting workflows to FDA, CPSC, or EPA as applicable. Critical for product safety compliance and recall management.
reference: CPSC Consumer Product Safety Act; FDA 21 CFR Part 314; EPA Reporting Requirements; ISO 9001

description: Timestamp when this return record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); ISO 8601
