The date on which the claim was formally withdrawn from consumer-facing use. Populated only when claim_status is 'withdrawn'. Critical for regulatory audit trail and litigation defense.
reference: FTC 16 CFR Part 260; CPSC; Veeva Vault

description: Free-text explanation for why a claim was withdrawn or rejected. Populated when claim_status is 'withdrawn' or 'under_review'. Captures regulatory challenge, substantiation failure, reformulation, or voluntary removal rationale.
reference: FTC 16 CFR Part 260; Veeva Vault Quality Documents

description: Primary key for category
Identifier of the immediate parent category in the hierarchy (null for top‑level).

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Category-level P&L reporting (e.g., Hair Care, Skin Care) is standard in consumer goods. Each product category maps to a profit center for category management decisions, trade promotion ROI analysis,
Standardized alphanumeric code identifying the category (e.g., GS1 segment).
Detailed textual description of the category, its purpose and typical product types.
Human‑readable name of the product category.
Current lifecycle status of the category.
Business classification indicating the nature of the category.
Timestamp when the category record was first created in the system.
Ordinal value used to order categories in UI listings.
Date when the category becomes active for product assignment.
Date when the category is retired or no longer usable (null if open‑ended).
Numeric depth of the category within the hierarchy (1 = top level).
Link to the representative image for the category.
Flag indicating whether the category has no child categories.
Marketing label applied to the category for promotional purposes.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the category record.

business_glossary_term: Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Registry ID
description: Unique surrogate primary key for each GTIN registry record in the Databricks Silver layer. Assigned by the platform upon ingestion from SAP S/4HANA MM or GS1 registration source.
Reference to the internal SKU master record to which this GTIN is assigned. A single SKU may have multiple GTINs across packaging hierarchy levels (each, inner pack, case, pallet).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; GS1 General Specifications

description: The date on which the GTIN was activated and made available for commercial use, EDI transactions, and retailer catalog publication. May differ from assignment_date when a GTIN is pre-assigned during NPD but activated at product launch.
reference: GS1 GTIN Management Standard; GS1 Sunrise Date Policy

description: The calendar date on which this GTIN was formally assigned to the SKU by the brand owner. Used for GS1 GTIN Management Standard compliance, audit trails, and PLM lifecycle tracking in SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: GS1 GTIN Management Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: The barcode symbology used to encode this GTIN on the physical product or packaging (e.g., UPC-A for consumer units, ITF-14 for cases, GS1-128 for logistic labels). Determines scanner compatibility at POS, warehouse, and DSD delivery points. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: UPC-A|EAN-13|EAN-8|ITF-14|GS1-128|GS1_DataBar|QR_Code — promote to reference product]
reference: GS1 General Specifications — Barcode Symbologies; ISO/IEC 15420; ISO/IEC 16388

description: The registered brand or trademark name under which the product is marketed to consumers. Used for retailer catalog syndication, Nielsen IQ market measurement reporting, and trade promotion management in Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud.
reference: GS1 Product Data Standard; Nielsen IQ Product Hierarchy

description: The 13-digit GS1 Global Location Number (GLN) identifying the legal entity that owns the brand and is responsible for the GTIN assignment. Required for GS1 Data Source (1WorldSync/Salsify) product data syndication and retailer onboarding.
reference: GS1 General Specifications — GLN; GS1 Data Source Standard

description: The standard number of cases stacked on one pallet (logistic unit). Used for transportation planning, warehouse slotting in Blue Yonder WMS, and pallet-level GTIN-14 assignment. Drives DRP and S&OP capacity calculations in SAP IBP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Packaging Hierarchy; Blue Yonder WMS; GS1 General Specifications

description: The single trailing check digit of the GTIN, calculated using the GS1 modulo-10 algorithm. Used to validate GTIN integrity during barcode scanning, EDI processing, and retailer catalog ingestion. Stored explicitly to support validation workflows.
reference: GS1 General Specifications — Check Digit Calculation; ISO/IEC 15420

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the product was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations, FDA import labeling, FTC country-of-origin rules, and retailer catalog submissions.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; FDA Import Labeling Requirements; FTC Country of Origin Rules; GS1 Product Data Standard

description: The timestamp when this GTIN registry record was first created in the system of record. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail, SOX compliance, and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404; ISO 8601; SAP S/4HANA Audit Log

description: The date on which the product data for this GTIN was first published to the GS1 data pool (GDSN). Used to track compliance with retailer new item setup timelines and GS1 sunrise date requirements.
reference: GS1 GDSN (Global Data Synchronization Network); GS1 Sunrise Date Policy

description: Indicates whether the full product data record for this GTIN has been published to a GS1-certified data pool (e.g., 1WorldSync, Salsify) for retailer catalog synchronization. True = published and available to subscribers; False = not yet published.
reference: GS1 Data Source Standard; 1WorldSync GDSN; Salsify PIM

description: The 13-digit EAN-13 barcode value used for international retail scanning and cross-border trade. Populated when gtin_format is GTIN-13. Required for EU, APAC, and LATAM retailer catalog submissions and EDI transactions.

Indicates whether this GTIN is set up and approved for EDI transactions (850 Purchase Order, 856 ASN, 832 Price/Sales Catalog) with trading partners. False when the GTIN is pending retailer item setup or has failed EDI validation. Managed in SAP S/4HANA SD and Oracle Cloud EDI.
reference: GS1 EDI Standards; ANSI X12 Transaction Sets 850/856/832; SAP S/4HANA SD

business_glossary_term: GS1 Global Product Classification (GPC) Brick Code
description: The 8-digit GS1 Global Product Classification (GPC) Brick Code that categorizes the product into a standardized product segment, family, class, and brick. Required for GS1 data pool submissions and used by retailers for category management and planogram (POG) optimization.
reference: GS1 Global Product Classification (GPC) Standard; GS1 Data Source

description: The GS1-assigned company prefix (6–12 digits) that identifies the brand owner within the GTIN structure. Issued by GS1 US or a GS1 Member Organization. Determines the number of GTINs available to the company and is embedded in every GTIN assigned by the company.
reference: GS1 General Specifications; GS1 Company Prefix Licence Agreement

description: The name of the GS1 Member Organization (e.g., GS1 US, GS1 UK, GS1 Germany) through which the company prefix was licensed. Relevant for multi-national CPG companies that hold prefixes from multiple GS1 member organizations across different markets.
reference: GS1 General Specifications; GS1 Member Organization Network

description: The reference number or confirmation identifier issued by GS1 (or a GS1 Member Organization) upon formal registration of this GTIN in the GS1 registry. Used for audit, dispute resolution, and regulatory submissions requiring proof of GS1 registration.
reference: GS1 GTIN Management Standard; GS1 Registry Platform

description: Indicates whether this GTIN has been published to the GS1 Registry Platform (formerly GEPIR) and is visible to trading partners and retailers for catalog synchronization. True = published; False = not yet published or withheld.
reference: GS1 Registry Platform; GS1 GTIN Management Standard

description: Identifies the GTIN format variant: GTIN-8 (EAN-8 short barcode), GTIN-12 (UPC-A, North American standard), GTIN-13 (EAN-13, international standard), or GTIN-14 (ITF-14, used for cases and pallets). Determines barcode symbology and EDI transaction set usage.
reference: GS1 General Specifications; GS1 US GTIN Management Standard

description: The full numeric GTIN string including the check digit. Supports GTIN-8, GTIN-12 (UPC-A), GTIN-13 (EAN-13), and GTIN-14 (shipping container/case) formats as defined by GS1 General Specifications. Used in barcode scanning, EDI transactions, and retailer catalog synchronization.

Single leading digit (0–9) used in GTIN-14 to indicate the packaging level of a trade item. A value of 0 indicates a consumer unit; values 1–8 indicate inner packs or cases; value 9 is reserved for variable-measure trade items. Applicable only when gtin_format is GTIN-14.
reference: GS1 General Specifications; GS1 GTIN-14 Allocation Rules

description: The number of inner packs (display shippers or multi-packs) contained within one case. Populated only when an intermediate inner-pack packaging level exists in the hierarchy. Used in SAP S/4HANA MM BOM and GS1 packaging hierarchy declarations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM BOM; GS1 General Specifications — Packaging Hierarchy

description: Indicates whether this GTIN represents the consumer-facing sellable unit (each/each pack) as opposed to an inner pack, case, or pallet. True = consumer unit eligible for POS scanning; False = logistic/trade unit. Drives OSA monitoring and POS data matching in Nielsen IQ and TradeEdge.
reference: GS1 General Specifications — Consumer Unit; Nielsen IQ POS Data Standard

description: Indicates whether this GTIN is currently eligible to be ordered by retail customers via EDI or Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud order management. False when the GTIN is retired, pending, or restricted to specific channels.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Order Management; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud

description: Indicates whether a valid, scannable barcode (UPC/EAN/ITF-14) has been verified and approved for this GTIN. False when the barcode artwork is pending verification or has failed GS1 barcode quality verification. Used in retail execution and DSD workflows in Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud.
reference: GS1 Barcode Verification Standard; ISO/IEC 15416; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud

description: Indicates whether this GTIN represents a variable-measure trade item (e.g., catch-weight products sold by weight). True = variable measure; False = fixed measure. Variable-measure GTINs use indicator digit 9 in GTIN-14 and require special POS and EDI handling per GS1 specifications.
reference: GS1 General Specifications — Variable Measure Trade Items; GS1 US Implementation Guide

description: The item reference portion of the GTIN, assigned by the brand owner to uniquely identify the product within the company prefix. Together with the company prefix and check digit, it forms the complete GTIN. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM material master EAN/UPC segment.
reference: GS1 General Specifications; SAP S/4HANA MM EAN Management

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this GTIN registry record in the system of record. Used for incremental data loads, change detection, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 404; ISO 8601; SAP S/4HANA Change Document

description: The unit of measure for the net content value (e.g., ml, L, g, kg, oz, fl_oz, ct, ea). Required for FDA label compliance, GS1 data pool submissions, and retailer catalog item setup. Follows GS1 Recommendation on Unit of Measure codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ml|L|g|kg|oz|fl_oz|ct|ea — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: GS1 Recommendation on Unit of Measure; FDA 21 CFR Part 101; FTC Fair Packaging and Labeling Act

description: The numeric net content quantity of the consumer unit (e.g., 400 for a 400ml shampoo bottle). Combined with net_content_uom to express the full net content declaration required on product labels per FDA and FTC labeling regulations.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101 — Food Labeling; FTC Fair Packaging and Labeling Act; GS1 Product Data Standard

description: Identifies the packaging hierarchy level to which this GTIN is assigned: each (consumer unit), inner pack (multi-pack or display shipper), case (master carton), or pallet (logistic unit). Drives EDI order management, warehouse scanning, and DRP/MRP planning in SAP S/4HANA and Blue Yonder WMS.
reference: GS1 General Specifications — Packaging Hierarchy; GS1 GTIN Allocation Rules

description: The current PLM lifecycle stage of the product associated with this GTIN: concept, development, commercialization, active (in market), end_of_life (phase-out initiated), or discontinued. Sourced from the PLM system and used to govern GTIN activation, EDI eligibility, and retailer catalog status.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Status; PLM Lifecycle Management Standard

description: The internal product category classification (e.g., Hair Care, Skin Care, Oral Care, Home Care) to which the SKU belongs. Aligns with the company's PLM product hierarchy and Nielsen IQ category definitions for market share reporting.
reference: Nielsen IQ Category Management; GS1 Product Classification (GPC)

description: Short consumer-facing product description associated with this GTIN, as published to retailer catalogs and GS1 data pools. Typically includes brand name, product name, variant, and net content (e.g., 'Brand X Moisturizing Shampoo 400ml'). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM material description.
reference: GS1 Product Data Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Description

description: Current lifecycle status of the GTIN registration: active (in use and published to trading partners), pending (assigned but not yet published), inactive (temporarily suspended), retired (permanently discontinued), or cancelled (assigned in error and voided). Governs EDI eligibility and retailer catalog synchronization.
reference: GS1 GTIN Management Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM EAN Status

description: The date on which the GTIN was retired or discontinued from active commercial use. Per GS1 GTIN Management Standard, a retired GTIN must not be reassigned for a minimum of 48 months. Null when the GTIN is still active.
reference: GS1 GTIN Management Standard — GTIN Reuse Rules

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this GTIN record was sourced: SAP_S4HANA (MM EAN management), Oracle_Cloud (procurement/supply chain), GS1_Registry (direct GS1 platform), or Manual (entered directly). Used for data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Oracle Cloud ERP; GS1 Registry Platform

description: The natural key or internal identifier of this GTIN record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP material number + EAN category combination). Used for delta-load reconciliation, deduplication, and lineage tracing in the Databricks Silver layer ingestion pipeline.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EAN/UPC Management; Oracle Cloud ERP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary market for which this GTIN is intended. A single SKU may have different GTINs for different target markets due to regulatory labeling, language, or formulation differences. Used in GS1 data pool syndication and Veeva Vault regulatory submissions.
reference: GS1 General Specifications — Target Market; ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; Veeva Vault Regulatory

description: The number of consumer units (eaches) contained within one case (master carton). Used in SAP S/4HANA MM for BOM/packaging hierarchy, DRP/MRP planning, and EDI order quantity validation. Also referenced in Blue Yonder WMS for receiving and putaway.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Packaging Hierarchy; GS1 General Specifications

description: The 12-digit UPC-A barcode value assigned to the consumer unit (each). Derived from the GTIN-12 and used primarily for North American retail POS scanning and retailer item setup. Populated only when gtin_format is GTIN-12.
reference: GS1 US UPC Implementation Guide; GS1 General Specifications

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each node in the product classification hierarchy. Serves as the primary key for this record in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

Reference to the immediate parent node in the product hierarchy tree. NULL for root-level nodes (e.g., Division). Enables recursive traversal of the Division → Category → Sub-Category → Brand → Sub-Brand → Product Form → SKU hierarchy.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Consumer goods category/division hierarchy nodes map to profit centers for brand and category P&L reporting. hierarchy.profit_center_code is a denormalized signal. A direct FK enables IBP-to-finance P
Date on which this hierarchy node was formally approved by the data steward or product management team. Supports PLM lifecycle governance and audit trail for regulatory submissions.
reference: Veeva Vault PLM; ISO 9001; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Code of the Brand-level ancestor node for this hierarchy node. Denormalized to enable brand-level P&L reporting, brand equity tracking, SOV (Share of Voice) analysis, and trade promotion planning at the brand level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Account Management

description: Code of the Category-level ancestor node for this hierarchy node. Denormalized to support category management, trade promotion planning (TPM), and demand forecasting without recursive hierarchy joins.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Trade Promotion

description: Distribution channels through which products in this hierarchy node are sold (e.g., 'Retail', 'E-Commerce', 'DTC', 'Wholesale', 'DSD'). Supports channel management, trade promotion planning, and distribution requirements planning (DRP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Retail|E-Commerce|DTC|Wholesale|DSD|Club|Drug — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud; TradeEdge Channel Visibility

description: Primary consumer segment targeted by products in this hierarchy node (e.g., 'Men 18-35', 'Families with Children', 'Premium Skincare Enthusiasts'). Used in brand marketing strategy, NPS (Net Promoter Score) analysis, and consumer engagement planning.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud; Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel

business_glossary_term: CPSC Regulated Flag (Consumer Product Safety Commission)
description: Indicates whether products in this hierarchy node are subject to CPSC (Consumer Product Safety Commission) regulations. True = product safety testing, labeling, and reporting requirements under CPSA apply.
reference: CPSC Consumer Product Safety Act (CPSA); 16 CFR

description: Timestamp when this hierarchy node record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with SOX financial data governance requirements.

Code of the top-level Division node to which this hierarchy node belongs (e.g., 'HPC' for Home & Personal Care, 'FOOD' for Food & Beverage). Denormalized for fast filtering in OLAP queries and S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) reporting without full hierarchy traversal.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; SAP IBP S&OP

description: Date from which this hierarchy node became effective and available for use in planning, trade promotion, and reporting systems. Supports temporal validity management and historical hierarchy reconstruction for trend analysis.

Date after which this hierarchy node is no longer active. NULL indicates an open-ended validity. Used for hierarchy versioning, portfolio rationalization, and ensuring discontinued nodes are excluded from forward-looking demand plans.

External partner or retailer-specific category code mapped to this hierarchy node (e.g., Walmart category code, Amazon browse node ID). Supports EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) transactions, retailer data synchronization, and VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory) programs.
reference: GS1 EDI Standards; Retail EDI Category Mapping

description: Applicable GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) standard for manufacturing products in this hierarchy node. Drives quality management requirements in Siemens Opcenter MES and Veeva Vault QMS. ISO 22716 applies to cosmetics; 21 CFR 111 to dietary supplements; 21 CFR 210/211 to pharmaceuticals.
reference: ISO 22716; FDA 21 CFR 111; FDA 21 CFR 210/211; ISO 9001

description: GS1 Global Product Classification (GPC) brick-level code (8-digit numeric) aligned to this hierarchy node. Enables cross-industry product classification interoperability, EDI transactions, and retailer data synchronization via GS1 standards.

Descriptive name of the GS1 GPC brick code associated with this hierarchy node (e.g., 'Shampoo/Conditioner - Non-Medicated'). Supports regulatory submissions, retailer onboarding, and GS1 data pool synchronization.

Numeric depth of this node within the product classification hierarchy. Level 1 = Division, Level 2 = Category, Level 3 = Sub-Category, Level 4 = Brand, Level 5 = Sub-Brand, Level 6 = Product Form, Level 7 = SKU (Stock Keeping Unit). Used for OLAP drill-down and aggregation logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; GS1 GPC

business_glossary_term: IBP Planning Level Flag (Integrated Business Planning)
description: Indicates whether this hierarchy node is a designated planning level in SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning) for demand forecasting and S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning). True = this node is an active planning aggregation point.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning; S&OP Process

business_glossary_term: INCI Applicable Flag (International Nomenclature of Cosmetic Ingredients)
description: Indicates whether products in this hierarchy node require INCI (International Nomenclature of Cosmetic Ingredients) ingredient declaration on labeling. True = cosmetic/personal care products subject to EU Cosmetics Regulation and FDA cosmetic labeling requirements.
reference: EU Cosmetics Regulation No 1223/2009; FDA 21 CFR Part 701; INCI

description: IRI (now Circana) proprietary category code mapped to this hierarchy node. Supports market share reporting, TDP (Total Distribution Points) analysis, and SOM (Share of Market) calculations using IRI syndicated data.

Indicates whether this node is a terminal leaf in the hierarchy (True = no children exist, typically the SKU level). Used to filter for assignable product nodes in demand forecasting, inventory management, and trade promotion planning.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: ISO 639-1 language code for the node_name and node_description fields (e.g., 'en', 'fr', 'de', 'es'). Supports multi-language product hierarchy management for global CPG/FMCG operations and localized reporting.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM Language-Dependent Texts

description: Business label for the hierarchy depth of this node. Enumerated values correspond to the CPG/FMCG standard classification spine: Division, Category, Sub-Category, Brand, Sub-Brand, Product Form, SKU (Stock Keeping Unit). Drives display logic in reporting tools and S&OP dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Division|Category|Sub-Category|Brand|Sub-Brand|Product Form|SKU — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP Demand Planning Hierarchy; Nielsen IQ Category Taxonomy

description: Indicates whether this hierarchy node is included in external market share reporting via Nielsen IQ or IRI/Circana syndicated data. True = node is mapped to a syndicated data category for SOM (Share of Market) and ACV (All Commodity Volume) reporting.

Nielsen IQ proprietary category code mapped to this hierarchy node. Enables direct linkage between internal product classification and Nielsen IQ retail measurement data for market share reporting, ACV (All Commodity Volume) analysis, and competitive benchmarking.
reference: Nielsen IQ Retail Measurement Service Category Taxonomy

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this hierarchy node within its level. Used as the business key in SAP S/4HANA MM product group structures and referenced in EDI transactions and trade promotion planning systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; GS1 GPC

description: Extended business description of the hierarchy node providing additional context about the product grouping, its scope, and intended use in classification. Supports regulatory categorization and PLM documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Veeva Vault PLM

description: Human-readable business name of this hierarchy node (e.g., 'Personal Care', 'Shampoo', 'Head & Shoulders', 'Classic Clean'). Used in OLAP drill-down reports, trade promotion planning, and market share dashboards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; Nielsen IQ Category Taxonomy

description: Current lifecycle status of this hierarchy node. 'active' nodes are available for use in planning, trade promotion, and reporting. 'discontinued' nodes are retained for historical analysis. 'pending' nodes are awaiting approval in the PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) workflow.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Veeva Vault PLM Lifecycle

description: Materialized full path string from root to this node using a delimiter (e.g., 'Consumer Goods / Personal Care / Hair Care / Shampoo / Head & Shoulders / Classic Clean'). Enables fast string-based filtering and display in BI tools without recursive joins.

Current stage of the product node in the PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) lifecycle. Drives NPD (New Product Development) workflows, portfolio rationalization decisions, and demand planning assumptions in SAP IBP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|development|launch|growth|mature|decline|discontinued — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Veeva Vault PLM; SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 9001 Product Lifecycle

description: Indicates whether products in this hierarchy node are subject to EU REACH Regulation (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals). True = chemical substance registration and SDS (Safety Data Sheet) requirements apply.

Identifies the primary regulatory framework governing products classified under this hierarchy node (e.g., 'FDA OTC Drug', 'FDA Cosmetic', 'EPA Pesticide', 'CPSC Consumer Product', 'EU Cosmetics Regulation'). Drives compliance workflows in Veeva Vault and regulatory labeling requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA OTC Drug|FDA Cosmetic|EPA Pesticide|CPSC Consumer Product|EU Cosmetics Regulation|REACH|ISO 22716|IFRA — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR; EU Cosmetics Regulation No 1223/2009; EPA FIFRA; CPSC; EU REACH Regulation

description: Numeric sequence controlling the display order of sibling nodes within the same parent. Used to enforce business-defined ordering in planograms (POG), category management reports, and trade promotion planning interfaces.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this hierarchy node was sourced (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM, Veeva Vault PLM, Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud). Supports data lineage tracking and Silver Layer reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAP_S4|ORACLE_ERP|VEEVA|SALESFORCE_CGC|SAP_IBP|NIELSEN|MANUAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Native identifier of this hierarchy node in the originating operational system (e.g., SAP product hierarchy node ID, Veeva Vault record ID). Enables reverse traceability from the Silver Layer back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; Enterprise Data Lineage

description: Indicates whether products in this hierarchy node carry a sustainability certification (e.g., FSC, RSPO, ISO 14001). Supports ESG reporting, sustainable sourcing procurement decisions, and consumer-facing sustainability claims.
reference: FSC (Forest Stewardship Council); RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil); ISO 14001

description: Tax classification code assigned to this hierarchy node for VAT/GST determination in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO. Drives correct tax treatment in sales orders, invoicing, and financial reporting across jurisdictions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Determination; SOX Compliance

description: Indicates whether products under this hierarchy node are eligible for trade promotion activities (TPM/TPO). Drives eligibility rules in Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Trade Promotion Management and ROI (Return on Investment) reporting.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud TPM; TPO Process

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this hierarchy node record in the Silver Layer. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance.

Monotonically incrementing version counter for this hierarchy node record. Incremented each time the node definition is materially changed (e.g., reclassification, rename, regulatory framework update). Supports SCD Type 2 history management and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Change Documents; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Primary key for material

Foreign key linking to procurement.approved_supplier_list. Business justification: Each raw material or packaging material must reference its approved supplier list entry for GMP compliance and quality control. Material master data in consumer goods always links to ASL for sourcing

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Raw and packaging materials are assigned to GL accounts (inventory valuation accounts) in consumer goods ERP. material.standard_cost signals valuation need. This FK supports material ledger postings,

Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_plan. Business justification: Raw materials and packaging materials each have dedicated incoming quality inspection plans in consumer goods manufacturing. QA links each material to its inspection plan for GMP-compliant incoming go
Self-referencing FK on material (parent_material_id)

Foreign key linking to regulatory.restricted_substance. Business justification: Raw and packaging materials must be screened against restricted substance lists during procurement and product development (REACH, RoHS, Prop 65). This FK enables material compliance screening — a nam

Foreign key linking to regulatory.sds. Business justification: Raw materials require Safety Data Sheets for EHS compliance, safe handling, storage, and transport (OSHA HazCom, EU CLP/REACH). A material-level SDS FK enables EHS teams to retrieve the correct SDS fo

Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Raw material and packaging material qualification requires each material to reference its quality specification. QA and procurement use this link to enforce supplier material acceptance criteria durin

Foreign key linking to procurement.spend_category. Business justification: Materials are classified into procurement spend categories for strategic sourcing, supplier consolidation, and budget management. The existing material_group plain attribute is a denormalized proxy fo

Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: Material master records are linked to governing supplier contracts for price determination, lead time commitments, and compliance terms. MRP and procurement planning in consumer goods use this link to
Identifier of the primary supplier for the material.
Allergen declarations associated with the material, if applicable.
Standard unit of measure used for inventory and transactions.
Brand under which the material is marketed.
Three‑letter ISO code of the country where the material is produced.
Date and time when the material record was first created in the system.
Three‑letter ISO currency code for the standard cost.
13‑digit European article number for the material.
Date when the material becomes effective for business use.
Date when the material is retired or no longer valid (null if open‑ended).
14‑digit global identifier assigned to the material for trade and retail purposes.
Indicates whether the material is classified as hazardous under safety regulations.
Date and time of the most recent update to the material record.
Current stage of the material within its product lifecycle.
Internal stock‑keeping unit or catalogue code that uniquely identifies the material within the enterprise.
Free‑form textual description of the material, including usage notes.
Human‑readable name of the material used in catalogs and reports.
Current lifecycle state of the material.
Category describing the nature of the material within the product hierarchy.
Primary packaging format for the material.
Current compliance status of the material with relevant regulations.
Maximum number of days the material can be stored before expiration.
Default cost price of the material used for costing and pricing calculations.
Optimal storage temperature for the material in degrees Celsius.
Factor to convert from the material's base UOM to alternative units.
12‑digit UPC code used primarily in North America.
Typical volume of the material per unit, expressed in liters.
Net weight of a single unit of material expressed in kilograms.

Unique surrogate identifier for each brand master record in the Consumer Goods portfolio. Primary key for the product_brand dimension, referenced by all downstream domains including marketing, trade promotion, and financial reporting.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Brand A&P (advertising and promotion) budgets are tracked against dedicated brand cost centers in consumer goods. This FK enables brand marketing spend reporting, budget vs. actual variance analysis,

Foreign key linking to regulatory.jurisdiction. Business justification: Brand trademark registrations and regulatory classifications are jurisdiction-specific. This FK replaces the denormalized trademark_jurisdiction plain attribute, enabling brand legal and regulatory te

Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Align internal product_brand records with marketing brand for unified brand strategy and reporting; brand_code is redundant once linked.

Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: product_brand has a primary_category (STRING) field that is a string reference to the product category. The category table is the authoritative master for product categories. Adding primary_category_i

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Brand-level P&L is a core consumer goods reporting requirement. Each product brand maps to a profit center for brand equity tracking, A&P spend allocation, and brand contribution margin reporting. Dom

Classification of the brand's role within the overall brand architecture strategy. Master brands are umbrella identities; sub-brands extend a master brand; endorsed brands carry a parent endorsement; standalone brands operate independently; house_of_brands indicates a portfolio brand with no visible corporate parent.
reference: GS1 Global Data Dictionary; Nielsen IQ Brand Hierarchy

description: Official consumer-facing name of the brand as registered and used in marketing, packaging, and trade communications. This is the primary human-readable identifier for the brand across all channels including retail, e-commerce, and DTC.
reference: FTC Advertising and Labeling Guidelines; GS1 Global Data Dictionary

description: Current lifecycle status of the brand within the Consumer Goods portfolio. Active brands are fully marketed and distributed; dormant brands are temporarily inactive; divested brands have been sold or licensed out; discontinued brands are permanently retired; pipeline brands are in NPD/R&D pre-launch phase.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PLM Brand Lifecycle; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud

description: Market positioning tier of the brand within the CPG/FMCG portfolio, indicating price-value positioning relative to competition. Drives RSP/MSRP strategy, trade promotion investment levels, and retail channel prioritization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: premium|mass|value|super_premium|private_label — promote to reference product if tiers expand]
reference: Nielsen IQ Brand Hierarchy Standards; SAP IBP Portfolio Planning

description: Timestamp when the brand master record was first created in the system of record. Provides the audit trail entry point for the brand's data lifecycle. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Primary channel through which the brand's products are distributed to consumers. Retail covers brick-and-mortar stores; DTC (Direct to Consumer) covers brand-owned channels; DSD (Direct Store Delivery) covers direct distribution; omnichannel covers integrated multi-channel distribution.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Channel Management; TradeEdge Channel Visibility

description: Date on which the brand was officially divested, sold, or licensed to a third party. Populated only when brand_status is 'divested'. Required for SOX financial reporting and portfolio restructuring audit trails.
reference: SOX Section 302; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

business_glossary_term: European Article Number (EAN) / GS1 Company Prefix
description: GS1 company prefix assigned to the brand owner, forming the first digits of all EAN/GTIN barcodes for products under this brand. Enables GS1-compliant product identification and EDI transactions across the global supply chain.
reference: GS1 Global Standards; GS1 Company Prefix Registry

description: Methodology or framework used to measure and track brand equity over time. Determines which external measurement partner (Nielsen IQ, Kantar BrandZ, Ipsos) or internal scorecard approach is applied for brand health KPI reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nielsen_brand_health|kantar_brandz|ipsos_brand_value|internal_scorecard|bav|none — promote to reference product if methodologies expand]
reference: Nielsen IQ Brand Measurement; ISO 10668 Brand Valuation

description: Geographic reach of the brand's commercial operations. Global brands are sold in multiple continents; regional brands operate within a defined multi-country region; national brands are limited to a single country; local brands serve sub-national markets; export_only brands are manufactured domestically but sold exclusively in foreign markets.
reference: GS1 Global Standards; SAP IBP Supply Planning

description: Reference number or identifier for the GMP certification applicable to the brand's manufacturing operations. Required for FDA-regulated brands (21 CFR Parts 110/111/210/211) and cosmetics brands under ISO 22716. Stored in Veeva Vault Quality Documents.
reference: FDA 21 CFR; ISO 22716 GMP for Cosmetics; ISO 9001

description: Indicates whether the brand is operated under a licensing agreement from a third-party brand owner rather than being wholly owned by the Consumer Goods entity. Licensed brands have different royalty accounting treatment in Oracle Cloud ERP Financials and different regulatory responsibility structures.
reference: SOX Section 302; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the brand master record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline, audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring.

Exact calendar date on which the brand was first commercially available to consumers. More precise than brand_launch_year; used for anniversary campaigns, brand lifecycle milestone tracking, and NPD stage-gate reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PLM; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud

description: Calendar year in which the brand was first commercially launched in any market. Used for brand age analytics, heritage positioning, and portfolio vintage analysis in brand equity tracking.

Date on which the brand licensing agreement expires, applicable only when is_licensed_brand is True. Critical for contract renewal planning, financial risk management, and SOX disclosure requirements. Null for wholly-owned brands.
reference: SOX Section 302; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials; IFRA (for fragrance brand licenses)

business_glossary_term: Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) Currency Code
description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the MSRP/RSP defined at the brand level. Provides the currency context for brand-level pricing strategy and trade promotion ROI calculations.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing; Oracle Cloud ERP

description: Indicates whether active NPS (Net Promoter Score) consumer sentiment tracking is configured for this brand. When True, the brand is enrolled in periodic consumer surveys and NPS measurement programs linked to the consumer domain's nps_response product.
reference: Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud CRM

description: Internal business division or strategic business unit (SBU) within the Consumer Goods organization that owns and manages the brand. Used for financial reporting, EBITDA attribution, and S&OP planning alignment in SAP S/4HANA CO module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling); Oracle Cloud ERP Financials; SOX

description: Legal entity name of the company or subsidiary that owns the brand trademark and bears regulatory responsibility for the brand. Critical for SOX financial reporting, regulatory submissions to FDA/EPA/CPSC, and inter-company transfer pricing.

Brand code of the parent or umbrella brand for sub-brands and brand extensions. Enables hierarchical brand architecture modeling (e.g., a skincare sub-brand under a master personal care brand). Null for top-level master brands with no parent.
reference: GS1 Global Data Dictionary; SAP S/4HANA Brand Hierarchy

description: Current stage of the brand in the PLM lifecycle framework. Drives resource allocation, marketing investment levels, and supply chain planning priorities. Concept and development stages are pre-commercial; growth through decline stages are active commercial phases; end_of_life triggers discontinuation planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|development|launch|growth|maturity|decline|end_of_life — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA PLM; SAP IBP Demand Planning

description: Internal strategic statement articulating the brand's unique value proposition, target audience, competitive frame of reference, and key differentiators. Confidential business strategy document used by marketing and trade promotion teams for campaign alignment.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Brand Management; FTC Advertising Guidelines

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary market or country of origin for the brand. Used for regulatory compliance (FDA, EU REACH), customs documentation, and market-level financial reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1; FDA 21 CFR; EU REACH Regulation

description: FDA/regulatory classification of the brand's product portfolio, determining applicable compliance frameworks. OTC drug brands require FDA monograph compliance; cosmetic brands require INCI ingredient declarations per ISO 22716; household chemical brands require EPA registration and SDS documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: otc_drug|cosmetic|food_beverage|household_chemical|medical_device|dietary_supplement|general_merchandise — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR; EPA Registration; CPSC; ISO 22716 GMP for Cosmetics; EU REACH

description: Abbreviated or shortened version of the brand name used in space-constrained contexts such as POS displays, planograms (POG), shelf labels, and reporting dashboards where the full brand name may not fit.

Primary social media handle or username for the brand (e.g., @BrandName on Instagram/Twitter/TikTok). Used for SOV (Share of Voice) measurement, digital marketing analytics, and consumer engagement tracking in Nielsen IQ and Salesforce.
reference: FTC Advertising and Labeling Guidelines; Nielsen IQ Digital Measurement

description: Native brand identifier as stored in the originating operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA brand master ID, Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud brand record ID). Enables reverse lookup and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and source systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Brand Master; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this brand master record originates. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and conflict resolution when multiple systems contribute brand attributes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud; Oracle Cloud ERP; Veeva Vault

description: Primary sustainability or environmental certification held by the brand, indicating compliance with responsible sourcing and environmental management standards. FSC covers sustainable forestry; RSPO covers sustainable palm oil; ISO 14001 covers environmental management systems.
reference: FSC Forest Stewardship Council; RSPO Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil; ISO 14001

description: Primary consumer demographic or psychographic segment the brand is designed to serve (e.g., Millennial Families, Gen Z Beauty Enthusiasts, Health-Conscious Adults 35-55). Drives brand positioning, media SOV allocation, and NPS/CLTV measurement segmentation.
reference: Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud CRM

description: Indicates whether the brand is eligible for trade promotion activities managed through TPM/TPO systems (Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud). Brands marked False may be excluded from promotional funding pools, Hi-Lo pricing events, and EDLP programs.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud TPM; SAP IBP S&OP

description: Official trademark registration number or reference identifier issued by the relevant intellectual property authority (e.g., USPTO, EUIPO, WIPO). Used for legal protection verification, brand licensing agreements, and regulatory labeling compliance.
reference: FTC Advertising and Labeling Guidelines; WIPO Madrid System

description: Official consumer-facing website URL for the brand. Used for DTC channel management, digital marketing attribution, and e-commerce integration. Referenced in Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud for omnichannel campaign tracking.
reference: FTC Advertising and Labeling Guidelines; GDPR Article 13

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each SKU record in the product master. Primary key for the sku data product, referenced by all downstream domains as the authoritative product identifier.
Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: SKU belongs to a product category; linking SKU to category eliminates silo and consolidates category data.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation report requires each SKU to be assigned a cost_center for expense tracking and product profitability analysis.

business_glossary_term: Default Supply Network Node Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Required for assigning each SKU to its primary distribution node used in supply planning, ATP calculations, and network optimization.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AR invoice posting uses a default sales GL account per SKU; linking enables automated revenue posting and audit trails.
Foreign key linking to product.product_hierarchy. Business justification: sku should be linked to its product hierarchy for classification; no existing column, so new FK added.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_facility. Business justification: CAPACITY PLANNING: Assign each SKU to its primary manufacturing facility for production capacity allocation, cost per facility, and regulatory reporting.
Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand performance reports require each SKU to be linked to its marketing brand for equity and spend analysis.
Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Primary Supplier Assignment for each SKU is required for sourcing strategy, cost analysis, and regulatory compliance reports.

Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_site. Business justification: Designating a primary supplier site per SKU supports logistics planning, VMI inventory control, and OTIF performance tracking.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability statements map each SKU to a profit_center, enabling SKU‑level P&L reporting and margin analysis.

Consumer-facing color descriptor for the SKU (e.g., 'Blue', 'Clear', 'White'). Applicable to products where color is a differentiating attribute (e.g., hair color, cosmetics, cleaning products). Used in planogram execution and consumer-facing content.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 701; FTC Labeling Guidelines

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the product is manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations, trade compliance, and consumer labeling in many markets.
reference: GS1 Global Data Dictionary; FDA 21 CFR; ISO 3166-1; WTO Rules of Origin

description: Timestamp when the SKU master record was first created in the system of record (SAP S/4HANA). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and compliance with SOX record-keeping requirements.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the MSRP and standard cost fields (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Date on which the SKU was or is planned to be permanently discontinued from the portfolio. Triggers end-of-life processes in supply chain, manufacturing, and trade. Null if the SKU is still active.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; Veeva Vault PLM

description: 13-digit European Article Number (EAN-13) used in international retail markets outside North America for barcode scanning and product identification. Registered with GS1.
reference: GS1 General Specifications; EU REACH Regulation

business_glossary_term: First Expired First Out (FEFO) Threshold Percentage
description: Minimum remaining shelf life percentage (of total shelf life) required at the time of shipment to a customer or retailer. Below this threshold, the product is considered non-shippable. Used in Blue Yonder WMS and SAP IBP inventory optimization.
reference: Blue Yonder WMS; SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; GMP Guidelines

description: Total gross weight of the consumer unit including packaging in kilograms. Used in logistics freight calculation, transportation planning, and customs documentation. Maps to SAP MM material master gross weight field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; GS1 Global Data Dictionary; OSHA

description: GS1-standard Global Trade Item Number uniquely identifying the product in global trade. Encompasses UPC-A (12-digit), EAN-13 (13-digit), and ITF-14 (14-digit) formats. Used for barcode scanning, EDI transactions, and retailer product listings.
reference: GS1 General Specifications; FDA 21 CFR Part 830

business_glossary_term: International Nomenclature of Cosmetic Ingredients (INCI) Declaration
description: Full INCI (International Nomenclature of Cosmetic Ingredients) ingredient list as declared on product packaging, listed in descending order of concentration. Mandatory for cosmetic and personal care products under FDA and EU regulations. Stored in Veeva Vault.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 701.3; EU Cosmetics Regulation 1223/2009; INCI Nomenclature; ISO 22716

description: Indicates whether the SKU is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, or transportation documentation (e.g., Safety Data Sheet / SDS). Triggers compliance workflows in logistics and warehouse management.
reference: OSHA Hazard Communication Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200; EPA Regulations; EU REACH Regulation

description: Indicates whether the primary packaging of the SKU is designed to be recyclable. Used in sustainability reporting, ESG disclosures, and consumer-facing labeling claims per FTC Green Guides.
reference: FTC Green Guides 16 CFR Part 260; ISO 14001; EU Packaging Directive

description: Indicates whether the SKU is subject to regulatory approval or registration requirements (e.g., FDA OTC drug, EPA-registered disinfectant, CPSC-regulated product). Triggers regulatory submission workflows in Veeva Vault.
reference: FDA 21 CFR; EPA Registration; CPSC Consumer Product Safety Act; Veeva Vault Regulatory

description: Indicates whether the SKU meets the company's sustainability criteria, such as FSC-certified packaging, RSPO-certified palm oil, or ISO 14001-aligned manufacturing. Used in sustainability reporting and ESG disclosures.
reference: FSC Forest Stewardship Council; RSPO Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil; ISO 14001

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the SKU master record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline, audit trail, and data quality monitoring.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001

description: Date on which the SKU was first commercially available for sale in any market. Used in new product development (NPD) tracking, demand planning baseline establishment, and trade promotion planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; Veeva Vault PLM

description: Current stage of the SKU in the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) framework (e.g., concept, development, launch, growth, mature, decline, discontinued). Governs which business processes are active for the SKU. Managed in Veeva Vault PLM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|development|launch|growth|mature|decline|discontinued — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Veeva Vault PLM; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Status; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) in the base currency. Represents the recommended consumer selling price (RSP) set by the manufacturer. Used in trade promotion planning (TPM), pricing strategy, and retailer negotiations. Confidential commercial data.
reference: FTC Pricing Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing; Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud

description: Numeric net content quantity of the product as declared on packaging (e.g., 500, 1.5, 200). Must be paired with net_content_uom. Regulated by FDA and FTC for accurate labeling. Used in COGS calculation and logistics weight/volume planning.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101; FTC Fair Packaging and Labeling Act; GS1 Global Data Dictionary

description: Unit of measure for the net content quantity (e.g., ml, L, g, kg, oz, fl oz, ct). Aligned with GS1 UN/CEFACT unit codes. Required for regulatory labeling compliance and logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ml|L|g|kg|oz|fl oz|ct|units — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: GS1 UN/CEFACT Unit Codes; FDA 21 CFR Part 101; FTC Fair Packaging and Labeling Act

description: Net weight of the product content excluding packaging in kilograms. Used in COGS calculation, regulatory labeling, and customs declarations. Maps to SAP MM material master net weight field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; FDA 21 CFR Part 101; GS1 Global Data Dictionary

description: Number of individual consumer units contained within a single sellable pack (e.g., 1 for single unit, 2 for twin pack, 6 for multipack). Used in trade promotion planning (TPM), pricing, and distribution requirements planning (DRP).
reference: GS1 General Specifications; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: Material type of the primary consumer packaging (e.g., plastic, glass, aluminum, paper, cardboard). Used in sustainability reporting, EU REACH compliance, and packaging specification management in Veeva Vault. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: plastic|glass|aluminum|paper|cardboard|composite|biodegradable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: EU REACH Regulation; ISO 14001; FSC; RSPO; Veeva Vault

description: Strategic classification of the SKU within the brand portfolio (e.g., core, strategic, tail, innovation, seasonal, limited_edition). Used in S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) prioritization, resource allocation, and SKU rationalization decisions.
reference: SAP IBP S&OP; Nielsen IQ Retail Measurement

description: Physical or chemical form of the product (e.g., liquid, powder, gel, cream, spray). Critical for manufacturing process routing in Siemens Opcenter MES, packaging specification, and regulatory classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: liquid|powder|gel|cream|foam|spray|bar|tablet|capsule|wipe|stick|sheet — promote to reference product]
reference: ISO 22716 Good Manufacturing Practices for Cosmetics; FDA 21 CFR

description: Full consumer-facing product name as it appears on packaging and in retail listings. Includes brand, variant, and size descriptors (e.g., 'Brand X Moisturizing Body Wash Fresh Scent 500ml'). Used in marketing, trade, and regulatory submissions.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 701; FTC Labeling Guidelines

description: Regulatory classification of the SKU determining the applicable regulatory framework and labeling requirements (e.g., cosmetic, OTC drug, household chemical, food). Drives compliance workflows in Veeva Vault and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cosmetic|otc_drug|household_chemical|food|dietary_supplement|medical_device|general_merchandise — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR; EPA Registration; CPSC; EU REACH Regulation; Veeva Vault Regulatory

description: Consumer-facing scent or fragrance descriptor for the SKU (e.g., 'Fresh', 'Lavender', 'Unscented'). Relevant for personal care and household products. Used in consumer engagement, marketing, and IFRA fragrance compliance tracking.
reference: IFRA International Fragrance Association Standards; FDA 21 CFR Part 701

description: Abbreviated product description (typically 40–80 characters) used in ERP transactions, warehouse management systems, and EDI communications where full product names are truncated. Maps to SAP MM short text field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; GS1 Global Data Dictionary

description: Internal alphanumeric code assigned to the SKU within the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA Material Number). Serves as the primary business identifier used in procurement, manufacturing, and sales transactions. Maps to SAP MM material number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; GS1 Global Data Dictionary

description: Current operational status of the SKU in the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA material status). Controls whether the SKU can be procured, manufactured, sold, or shipped. 'blocked' prevents all transactions; 'pending_launch' indicates pre-commercialization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; GS1 Global Data Dictionary

description: Standard cost per consumer unit used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation, financial reporting, and profitability analysis. Set annually during standard cost roll in SAP S/4HANA CO. Confidential financial data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Product Costing; SOX Financial Reporting; EBITDA Reporting

description: Required storage conditions for the SKU throughout the supply chain (e.g., ambient, refrigerated, frozen, controlled_temperature). Drives warehouse slotting in Blue Yonder WMS, transportation lane requirements, and regulatory compliance.
reference: ISO 22716 GMP for Cosmetics; FDA 21 CFR; Blue Yonder WMS

description: Sub-brand or product line within the parent brand (e.g., 'Dove Men+Care', 'Tide PODS'). Enables granular brand portfolio analysis and targeted trade promotion management (TPM).
reference: Nielsen IQ Retail Measurement; SAP IBP Demand Planning

description: Second-level product classification within the category (e.g., 'Shampoo', 'Conditioner', 'Detergent Pods'). Supports granular category management, demand planning in SAP IBP, and retail execution reporting.
reference: GS1 Global Product Classification (GPC); SAP IBP Demand Planning

description: Primary consumer demographic segment this SKU is designed and marketed for (e.g., adult, baby, male, female, professional). Drives consumer segmentation, marketing investment allocation, and shelf placement strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: adult|baby|child|teen|senior|unisex|male|female|professional|family — promote to reference product]
reference: FTC Advertising and Labeling Guidelines; Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel

description: Total shelf life of the SKU in days from the date of manufacture to the expiry date as declared on packaging. Used in FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management, Blue Yonder WMS configuration, and regulatory compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR; ISO 22716 GMP for Cosmetics; Blue Yonder WMS

description: 12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC-A) used primarily in North American retail for point-of-sale scanning and inventory management. Subset of GTIN. Registered with GS1 US.

Specific variant descriptor distinguishing this SKU from others within the same product line (e.g., 'Original', 'Sensitive Skin', 'Extra Strength', '2-in-1'). Used in PLM lifecycle management and consumer segmentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; Veeva Vault PLM

description: Volume of the consumer unit in millilitres, used for liquid and semi-liquid products. Supports logistics cube utilization calculations, warehouse slotting, and transportation planning.
reference: GS1 Global Data Dictionary; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: Unique identifier for the baseline volume record. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key reference to the product category for which baseline volume is calculated.

Foreign key linking to marketing.market_research_study. Business justification: CPG baseline volume models incorporate market research data (household panel studies, purchase frequency research). Linking baseline_volume to market_research_study documents the research inputs used

Foreign key linking to product.product_brand. Business justification: Baseline volumes in trade promotion management are computed and tracked at the product brand level for brand-level promotional lift analysis and annual planning. Brand managers review baseline trends
Foreign key reference to the SKU for which baseline volume is calculated.
Foreign key reference to the retailer or trade account for which baseline volume is calculated.
Boolean flag indicating whether the baseline volume estimate was manually overridden by an analyst (True) or is system-generated (False).
The date when the baseline volume estimate was approved for use in promotional lift calculations.
The statistical methodology used to calculate the baseline volume estimate (e.g., moving average, regression, Nielsen IQ panel, Bayesian structural time series).
The end date of the period for which this baseline volume estimate applies.
The start date of the period for which this baseline volume estimate applies.
The estimated baseline revenue for the SKU-retailer-period combination at regular pricing, absent promotional activity.
The current lifecycle status of the baseline volume record (e.g., draft, approved, active, archived, superseded, rejected).
The date and time when the baseline volume calculation was performed.
The estimated baseline sales volume in cases, representing expected sales absent any promotional activity.
The lower bound of the confidence interval for the baseline volume estimate, representing statistical uncertainty.
The upper bound of the confidence interval for the baseline volume estimate, representing statistical uncertainty.
The confidence level percentage for the confidence interval (e.g., 95.00 for 95% confidence).
The date and time when this baseline volume record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the baseline revenue amount.
The source system or data provider from which the baseline calculation data was obtained (e.g., POS, Nielsen IQ, IRI, internal sales, TradeEdge).
Three-letter ISO country code or geographic region code for which the baseline volume applies.
The number of historical weeks of data used in the baseline volume calculation methodology.
The date and time when this baseline volume record was last modified.
The date when the baseline volume estimate was last refreshed or recalculated.
A statistical measure of the baseline model accuracy, such as R-squared or Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), represented as a decimal value.
The version identifier of the baseline calculation model or algorithm used to generate this estimate.
Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or context related to the baseline volume calculation.
Boolean flag indicating whether statistical outliers were excluded from the baseline calculation (True) or included (False).
The business justification or reason provided by the analyst for manually overriding the system-generated baseline estimate.
The date and time when the analyst override was applied to the baseline estimate.
The number of promotional periods that were excluded from the historical data when calculating the baseline volume.
The multiplicative factor applied to adjust for seasonal patterns in the baseline volume calculation.

business_glossary_term: Trade Promotion Management (TPM) System Reference ID
description: External reference identifier from the source Trade Promotion Management system (e.g., Salesforce TPM) for traceability.
The multiplicative factor applied to adjust for long-term trend patterns in the baseline volume calculation.
The estimated baseline sales volume in units, representing expected sales absent any promotional activity.
Unique identifier for the promotional lift measurement record.

Foreign key linking to promotion.baseline_volume. Business justification: Lift measurement calculates incremental volume by comparing actual promoted volume against a statistical baseline. The baseline_volume entity is the authoritative source for the baseline estimate used

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: CPG integrated effectiveness reporting requires separating media-driven lift from trade-driven lift. Linking lift_measurement to the concurrent marketing campaign enables the 'trade vs. media contribu

Foreign key linking to marketing.consumer_segment. Business justification: CPG promotion analytics routinely measures lift by consumer segment (heavy vs. light buyers, loyalists vs. switchers). Linking lift_measurement to marketing-defined consumer segments enables segment-l

Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: Lift measurement is captured at the event execution level — a specific promotional event drives the incremental volume lift being measured. While lift_measurement already links to trade_promotion (the

Foreign key linking to inventory.oos_event. Business justification: Lift measurement methodology must identify and adjust for OOS events during the measurement period, as stockouts suppress observed promotional lift. Linking to the specific OOS record enables statisti
Reference to the specific retail store location where lift was measured, if applicable.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud - Retail Execution

description: Reference to the specific SKU for which promotional lift is measured.

Foreign key linking to finance.standard_cost. Business justification: Lift measurement calculates incremental_revenue and ROI metrics that require standard cost for margin calculations. Consumer Goods finance and commercial teams use standard cost to convert volume lift
Reference to the retailer or trade account where the promotion was executed.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud - Account Management

description: Reference to the trade promotion event for which lift is being measured.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud - Trade Promotion Management

description: The actual volume in units sold during the promotional period.
The specific method used to calculate the baseline volume for comparison.
The number of weeks of historical data used to establish the baseline trend.
The expected volume in units based on pre-promotion trend analysis, representing what would have sold without the promotion.
The percentage of incremental lift that came from other SKUs within the same brand or category, indicating internal sales transfer.
The lower bound of the confidence interval for the lift measurement, typically at 95% confidence level.
The upper bound of the confidence interval for the lift measurement, typically at 95% confidence level.
The statistical confidence level of the lift measurement, typically 90%, 95%, or 99%.
The date and time when this lift measurement record was first created in the system.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this record.
The date when the source data used for this lift measurement was extracted or made available.
The percentage discount offered during the promotion, calculated as ((regular price - promotional price) / regular price) * 100.
The additional volume lift observed in related non-promoted SKUs due to the promotion, representing positive spillover effects.
The percentage lift over baseline, calculated as (incremental lift units / baseline volume units) * 100.
The incremental volume in units attributable to the promotion, calculated as actual promoted volume minus baseline volume.
The incremental revenue generated by the promotion, calculated as incremental lift units multiplied by promotional price.
The date and time when this lift measurement record was last updated.
The data source used to measure promotional lift.

description: The statistical methodology used to calculate baseline and incremental lift.
Free-text notes documenting any anomalies, data quality issues, or special considerations for this lift measurement.
The current status of the lift measurement in the validation and approval workflow.
The end date of the week for which lift is measured.
The start date of the week for which lift is measured, typically aligned with retailer calendar weeks.
The p-value from the statistical test of lift significance, where values below 0.05 typically indicate statistical significance.
The volume decline observed in the weeks immediately following the promotion, indicating pantry loading or forward buying effects.
The actual selling price during the promotional period.
The regular non-promotional selling price used as baseline for price comparison.
Indicates whether the measured lift is statistically significant at the specified confidence level.

business_glossary_term: Trade Promotion Management (TPM) System Reference ID
description: The unique identifier for this lift measurement record in the source TPM system (Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud).
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud - Trade Promotion Management

description: The date and time when the lift measurement was validated and approved for use in ROI calculations.
Unique identifier for the post-event analysis record. Primary key for this analytical entity capturing promotion performance measurement at SKU-retailer-period granularity.

Foreign key linking to promotion.baseline_volume. Business justification: Post-event analysis computes incremental lift by comparing actual promoted volume against a statistical baseline. The baseline_volume entity is the authoritative source for the baseline estimate used

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: CPG post-event analysis explicitly isolates trade promotion contribution from concurrent marketing campaign contribution. A direct campaign_id enables the 'integrated post-event scorecard' report that

Foreign key linking to marketing.consumer_segment. Business justification: Post-event analysis in CPG is segmented by consumer segment to understand differential response rates. This segment-level breakdown is a standard deliverable in CPG post-event analysis reports and inf
Reference to the completed promotion event being analyzed. Links to the source promotion execution record in the TPM system.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud - Trade Promotion Management

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.oos_event. Business justification: Post-event analysis tracks oos_impact_estimated_units and oos_incident_count as plain attributes, indicating OOS data is consumed here. Linking to the actual oos_event record enables drill-down from p
Reference to the specific SKU that was promoted during this event. Enables SKU-level performance analysis and lift measurement.
reference: GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Standards

description: Foreign key linking to finance.standard_cost. Business justification: Post-event analysis calculates cost_per_incremental_case and promotional_roi, which require standard cost data. Consumer Goods analysts use standard cost to compute true promotional profitability. Dom
Reference to the retailer or trade partner where the promotion was executed. Enables retailer-level performance comparison and compliance tracking.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud - Account Management

description: Actual sales volume in units achieved during the promotional period. Core metric for calculating incremental lift and ROI.
Total trade spend actually incurred for this promotion event, including all accruals, deductions, and settlements. Used for actual ROI and GMROI calculation.
Date and time when the post-event analysis was completed and results finalized. Marks the transition to approved or archived status.
End date of the promotional period being analyzed. Completes the temporal boundary for performance measurement window.
Start date of the promotional period being analyzed. Defines the temporal boundary for lift measurement and baseline comparison.
Current lifecycle status of the post-event analysis. Tracks progression from initial draft through approval and archival.
Free-text commentary from the analyst conducting the post-event review. Captures qualitative insights, contextual factors, and recommendations for future planning.
Date and time when this post-event analysis was formally approved. Locks the record for reporting and TPO model training.
Lower bound of the statistical confidence interval for baseline estimate. Quantifies uncertainty in baseline calculation.
Upper bound of the statistical confidence interval for baseline estimate. Completes the uncertainty quantification for baseline.
Primary data source used for baseline estimation. Identifies whether baseline was derived from POS scan data, syndicated panels, or internal systems.

Statistical or analytical method used to calculate the baseline volume estimate. Critical for understanding confidence and reproducibility of lift measurements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: moving_average|exponential_smoothing|regression|causal_model|nielsen_iq|tradeedge|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Estimated baseline sales volume in units that would have occurred without the promotion. Foundation for incremental lift calculation.
Trade spend divided by incremental lift in case-equivalent units. Measures cost efficiency of generating incremental volume.
Date and time when this post-event analysis record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record. Ensures consistent financial reporting across markets.
As-of date for the sales data used in this analysis. Important for understanding data latency and potential for future revisions.
Indicates whether the retailer executed the agreed in-store display (end cap, shipper, pallet) as planned. True if compliant, False if not.
Indicates whether the retailer executed the agreed feature advertising (circular, flyer, digital ad) as planned. True if compliant, False if not.
Gross margin return on investment, calculated as incremental gross margin divided by actual trade spend. Measures profitability efficiency of promotional investment.
Percentage lift over baseline, calculated as (incremental lift units / baseline volume units) * 100. Enables comparison across SKUs and events of different scales.
Incremental sales volume in units attributable to the promotion, calculated as actual promoted volume minus baseline volume. Primary measure of promotional effectiveness.
Date and time when this post-event analysis record was last updated. Tracks iterative refinement during analysis workflow.
Categorical assessment of promotion performance used to feed TPO learning loops. Enables pattern recognition and future optimization.
Analytical approach used to isolate and measure promotional lift. Determines rigor and defensibility of lift claims.
Primary data source used to measure actual promoted volume and calculate lift. Critical for understanding data quality and measurement reliability.

business_glossary_term: Out of Stock (OOS) Impact Estimated Units
description: Estimated sales volume lost due to out-of-stock incidents during the promotion. Quantifies the opportunity cost of poor availability.
Number of out-of-stock incidents detected during the promotional period. Critical for understanding lost sales opportunity and execution quality.
Total trade spend budgeted for this promotion event at planning time. Baseline for variance analysis and ROI calculation.
Observed sales trend in the period immediately preceding the promotion. Contextualizes baseline and helps explain lift performance.
Indicates whether the retailer maintained the agreed promotional price throughout the event period. True if compliant, False if not.
Return on investment for this promotion, calculated as (incremental gross profit - actual trade spend) / actual trade spend. Primary financial effectiveness metric.
Strategic recommendation for how this promotion type should be treated in future planning cycles based on performance results.
Composite score (0-100) measuring retailer adherence to promotional execution requirements including pricing, display, feature, and inventory. Higher scores indicate better execution quality.
reference: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud - Retail Execution

description: Percentage of promoted inventory that sold through to consumers during the promotional period. Indicates promotional velocity and inventory efficiency.
Difference between actual and planned trade spend (actual minus planned). Highlights budget adherence and cost control effectiveness.
Unique identifier for the retailer compliance record. Primary key for this transactional compliance audit entity.
